Items.Title: 负载平衡器，以及 Power bi 中 Vnet 网关的故障排除问题 | Load balancers, and troubleshooting issues with the Vnet gateway in power bi
Items.Description:
我们很高兴为 Vnet 数据网关推出三项新功能。 发泄数据网关！

最近，我们添加了构建 Vnet 数据网关集群的功能。 可以在虚拟网络中创建数据网关组，以对在集群内运行的查询进行负载平衡，以防止出现单一故障源的可能性。

如果 Vnet 未使用，我们还添加了调整持续时间直到自动暂停的功能。 这使您可以确保您的 Vnet 网关在需要时处于运行状态。

您是否希望了解更多关于 Power BI 的深入知识和实践经验，例如 Power BI Desktop、体系结构、DAX、服务、移动应用、报表和 QA，以及行业用例以获得专业知识商业分析？ 您应该检查Power BI 认证。

我们还添加了帮助解决 VNet 数据网关问题的功能。 如果您遇到与用于连接的数据源的网络连接问题，您可以调查网关的数据网关。

虚拟数据网关集群的高可用性和负载平衡

可以通过为虚拟网络创建新的数据网关来创建高可用性集群。 组中存在多个网关可确保您的公司能够通过虚拟网络访问数据，并防止在本地访问数据资源时出现单一故障源的可能性。

具有多个网关的集群还可以提供负载转移——在负载平衡随机发生时选择网关。

有两种方法可以为网络构建虚拟数据网关。 第一种是使用多个网关创建一个集群来制作网关。 另一种选择是修改虚拟网络中现有数据网关的设置。

如果要创建全新的虚拟网络数据网关，首先必须通过 Power Platform 管理中心填写创建虚拟网络所需的详细信息。 然后，您将看到高级选项菜单。

您如何确保您的网关已准备好执行查询

虚拟网络网关将在一段时间不活动后自动停止。 当网关被自动阻塞时，网络重新打开大约需要两到三分钟。 自动暂停前的默认不活动时间设置为 30 分钟。 可以将此间隔增加到 24 小时。 没有办法让数据网关集群持续运行。

什么是虚拟数据网关故障排除？

故障排除数据包括以下信息：

以太网适配器信息：

提供有关网关的网络连接设置的详细信息。 此信息可用于确定 Internet 协议 (IP) 是否适用于它所在的子网。 此外，您可以检查您的域名系统 (DNS) 设置是否正常。

名称解析和测试网络连接：

提供通过 IP 或域名或端口测试端点和容器之间连接的能力。 如果测试失败，很可能是设备没有正确响应。 当 DNS 不响应地址或网络不可用时，可能会发生这种情况。 如果网络测试不成功，分配源的配置 IP 可能是个问题。



原文:

We are thrilled to unveil three new features for Vnet Data Gateway. Vent Data Gateway!

Recently, we added the ability to build Vnet Data Gateway clusters. It is possible to create groups of data gateways in a virtual network to load balance queries that run within the cluster to prevent the possibility of a single source of failure.

We have also added the ability to adjust the duration of time until auto-pause if the Vnet is not in use. This lets you make sure that your Vnet gateway is in operation when required.

Are you looking to know more about Power BI in-depth knowledge and hands-on experience with Power BI Desktop, Architecture, DAX, Service, Mobile Apps, Reports, and QA, to name a few, with industry use cases to gain expertise in Business Analytics? You should check for thePower BI Certification.

We have also added the ability to help troubleshoot issues with VNet Data Gateway. You can investigate your gateway's data gateway if you're experiencing issues with network connectivity with the data sources it has for connection.

High-availability and load-balancing a cluster of a virtual data gateway

It is possible to create high-availability clusters by creating a new data gateway for a virtual network. The presence of multiple gateways in the group guarantees that your company will be able to access data via the virtual network and prevent the possibility of a single source of failure for accessing data resources on-premises.

A cluster with multiple gateways can also provide load shifting—choosing the gateway when load balancing happens randomly.

There are two methods to build the virtual data gateways for the network. The first is to create a cluster using multiple gateways to make the gateway. The other alternative is to modify the settings of the existing data gateways in the virtual network.

If you're creating a brand new Virtual Network Data Gateway, you'll first have to fill in the required details for creating the virtual network through the Power Platform admin center. Then, you'll see the advanced options menu.

How do you ensure that your gateway is ready to execute queries

Virtual network gateways will automatically stop after a set period of inactivity. When the gateway is automatically blocked, it takes about two to three minutes for the network to re-open. The default period of inactivity before auto-pause is set at 30 minutes. It is possible to increase this interval up to 24 hours. There's no way to leave the data gateway cluster running continuously.

What is virtual Data gateway troubleshooting?

The troubleshooting data includes the following information:

Ethernet adapter information:

Provides details about the gateway's network connectivity settings. This information can be used to determine if Internet Protocol (IP) is appropriate for the subnet in which it is. Also, you can check whether your Domain Name System (DNS) setting is in order.

Name resolution and test network connection:

Offers the ability to test connectivity between the endpoint and the container via IP or the domain's name or port. If the tests fail, it's most likely that the device isn't responding correctly. This can happen when the DNS does not respond to an address or unavailable network. If the network test is unsuccessful, assigning the source's configuration IP is likely to be an issue.
Items.Author: pahome8199

Items.Title: 为客户服务代理提供可操作的客户反馈 | Actionable Customer Feedback for Customer Service Agents
Items.Description:
客户是任何企业的生命线。 如果您的客户满意，他们将为您的业务带来持续的收入。 但是，如果您的客户不满意，您的企业将很难生存。 因此，客户满意度对于您的企业生存和成功运营至关重要。

客户满意度不仅是优质产品的结果，也是您为客户提供的支持和服务水平。 研究表明， 90%的美国人认为客户服务是与公司开展业务的决定性因素。 假设您尽一切努力和投资为您的客户提供最好的服务。 您创建一个完善的客户服务系统，雇用员工，组建一个客户服务代表团队，并培训他们提供世界一流的服务。 但是您怎么知道您的客户服务代理是否完美地完成了他们的工作以及您的客户对服务是否满意？ 唯一的方法是收集客户反馈。

客户反馈为您提供有关客户对他们从您的组织获得的体验的想法和感受的宝贵信息。 收集反馈可以帮助您了解您的客户是否对所提供的服务感到满意。 让我们了解为什么需要收集有关客户服务代理的客户反馈。

为什么客户服务代理的客户反馈很重要？

您的客户服务代理是否及时响应？ 他们对您的客户是否热情友好？ 他们是否以正确的方式开展工作并为您的客户提供所需的支持？ 他们能够改善客户体验吗？ 关于客户服务代理的反馈为所有这些问题提供了答案。

要进一步了解收集有关客户服务代理的反馈的重要性，让我们探索它的一些优势。

客户服务代理的客户反馈的好处

  1. 它有助于衡量客户满意度
  2. 它有助于跟踪服务代理的性能
  3. 它有助于留住客户并防止客户流失
  4. 它有助于改善您的产品和服务
  5. 它有助于开发有效的流程

让我们回顾一下客户反馈的这些好处如何帮助您在业务中表现出色。

收集客户反馈的最大好处是它是衡量客户满意度的关键。 当您收集反馈时，您会了解您的客户是否以及在多大程度上对您的代理为他们提供的服务感到满意。

有关客户服务代理的反馈可帮助您了解他们的工作做得如何。 它使您能够查看他们是否可以为客户提供所需的支持。

客户反馈可帮助您确保您的座席及时响应客户的请求和疑虑，并尽最大努力满足他们。 它可以帮助您跟踪座席表现的改进，并了解哪些座席需要更多培训。

几次之后，不满意的客户最终会流失。 如果客户对您的服务不满意，客户反馈是了解这一点的方式。 当您了解这种不满时，您会努力提高客户满意度并改善体验。

当您尽最大努力，对负面反馈采取必要措施并告知客户您正在为他们做什么时，这会在很大程度上改变他们的看法。 这有助于留住客户并防止客户流失。 研究表明，将客户保留率提高 5% 可以将您的利润提高25% 到 95% 。

客户反馈可作为改进您的产品和服务的指南。 它可以帮助您识别产品和服务的优势和劣势，从而进一步帮助增强优势并克服劣势，从而改进您的产品和服务。

研究表明， 83%的客户对那些回应并解决他们问题的品牌更加忠诚。 因此，客户服务和支持流程对于有效至关重要。

客户反馈可帮助您识别这些流程中的漏洞并对其进行处理，以便为客户制定一个顺畅的流程来提出请求和疑虑并获得支持。

如何为服务代理收集和使用可操作的客户反馈

当您了解了收集有关客户服务代理的客户反馈的优势时，问题就出现了，即如何收集反馈并最有效地使用它，以便及时采取适当的行动并让您的企业从中获得最大利益。 让我们探索如何做到这一点。

收集和使用有关客户服务代理的客户反馈的方法

  1. 使用客户反馈调查收集反馈
  2. 为问题创建票证
  3. 发送自动客户反馈调查
  4. 在服务通信电子邮件中发送嵌入式调查
  5. 与客户服务代理共享调查结果

让我们学习如何收集和使用反馈以造福您的业务。

要收集和使用客户反馈，首要任务是向客户征求反馈。 向客户征求反馈的最佳方式是创建并向他们发送客户反馈调查。 您可以为此目的使用客户反馈软件。 一个好的客户反馈应用程序和软件允许您创建和发送多种类型的调查。

您还可以使用度量调查，这在衡量客户满意度、忠诚度和客户完成工作的感知努力方面非常有效。 流行的指标调查是：

一种。 核动力源
向客户提出了一个简单的问题。

此处，向客户提供 0 到 10 的度量标准以选择评级分数，其中 10 表示“最有可能”，0 表示“完全不可能”。 根据 NPS 调查结果，受访者分为三类：促进者、被动者和批评者。

发起人
是那些给 9 分和 10 分的人。他们被认为是最满意的忠诚客户，并且最有可能向他们的知名客户推荐该品牌。

被动者是那些提供 7 或 8 分的产品。他们通常是满意的客户，但往往会在获得更好的交易时尝试切换到其他品牌。

诋毁者是那些评分在 0 到 6 之间的人。他们被认为是不满意的客户，最有可能转向另一个品牌，甚至与知名品牌分享他们的糟糕经历，从而传播关于该品牌的坏口碑。

净推荐值是通过从推荐者的百分比中减去批评者的百分比来衡量的。 它可以介于 -100 到 100 之间。通常认为正 NPS 是好的，负 NPS 是坏的。

湾。 与客户服务代理的互动主要影响客户满意度和体验水平。 因此，衡量 CSAT 有助于了解客户对客户服务代理的满意程度。 向客户提出了一个简单的问题：

以不同的形式向客户提供五种评级选项，如不言自明的笑脸、星级或形容词，如优秀、好、一般、坏和差。

最终分数是通过找到平均分数来计算的，平均分数是通过将总分数除以受访者的数量来完成的。

消费电子展
该指标直接衡量您的客户服务代理在帮助您的客户并使他们的生活更轻松方​​面的工作情况。

在CES 调查中，向客户提出了一个简单的问题：

向客户提供了一个 7 级评级量表，其中包括“非常不同意”、“有些不同意”、“既不同意也不不同意”、“有点同意”和“非常同意”的选项。

使用有效的调查软件，您还可以根据您的行业和要求创建和修改其他类型的调查，例如客户反馈调查、患者反馈调查、学生反馈调查、员工反馈调查、客户反馈调查等。您可以通过以下方式发送这些调查多种渠道，如电子邮件、短信、网站、二维码、Android 平板电脑和智能手机。

您还可以选择使用客户提供的反馈创建 Zendesk 问题。 假设客户提供的负面反馈涉及对产品和服务的关注。 通过将调查软件与 Zendesk、Helpscout 和 Freshdesk 等软件集成，您可以选择要自动创建的问题和工单，负面反馈会引起关注。

这将帮助您有效地使用客户反馈，在正确的时间采取适当的措施来改善客户体验并关闭反馈循环。

要获取有关客户服务代理和支持流程的确切客户体验的信息，您可以使用自动化在正确的时间发送反馈调查。 您可以触发调查以及针对客户问题提出的票证。

您可以在诸如提出工单、更新工单以及最恰当地关闭工单等事件中触发调查，以了解解决问题的客户体验。

在为客户提供支持和服务的同时，也有很多情况下会进行电子邮件通信。 在这种情况下，您可以在服务通信电子邮件的正文中添加嵌入式调查。 电子邮件调查作为常规通信电子邮件的一部分到达客户时，更有可能得到回复。

在电子邮件中嵌入客户反馈调查非常简单。 您只需从调查应用程序中复制调查的 HTML 代码并将其粘贴到 Zendesk 的电子邮件中。 它将选择调查并将其显示在电子邮件正文中。 这些调查对于衡量每个接触点的服务代理的客户体验非常有益。 他们让您及时了解流程之间是否存在任何问题，以便您可以在事情恶化之前在正确的时间采取行动。

当您发送了反馈调查，并且客户已经回复了它，下一步就是对收到的反馈采取行动。 您是否知道77%的公司没有完善的系统来响应反馈？ 成为他们中的一员根本不是一个明智的决定，对吧！

因此，您应该制定一个有效的流程来响应客户反馈、采取行动并有效地关闭反馈循环。 您必须培训您的服务代理对客户反馈采取行动并改善体验。 为此，必须与您的代理分享客户的反馈，以便他们了解他们的表现以及他们的表现让客户不满意的地方。

为此，您应该使用共享选项，尤其是在您的反馈收集团队和客户服务代理不同的情况下。 此功能使您可以选择分享客户给出的评分及其评论。 您可以选择与代理共享其中一个或两个，以便他们知道哪里出了问题，他们应该采取什么行动，以及他们如何避免将来出现问题。

这将有助于改善座席的表现和客户体验。

结论
客户服务代理是您公司的门面。 很明显，要密切关注他们的表现以及他们与客户打交道的方式。 客户反馈是此信息的最佳来源，收集有关您的服务代理的反馈至关重要。

使用 Zonka 反馈，您可以创建任何类型的调查并通过多种渠道和方式发送。



原文:

Customers are the lifeline of any business. If your customers are happy, they will give continuous revenue for your business. But if your customers are unhappy, it will be hard for your business to survive. So Customer Satisfaction is vital for your business to survive and run successfully.

Customer Satisfaction is not only the outcome of a good product but also the level of support and service you provide to your customers. Researches show that 90% of Americans consider Customer Service a deciding factor in doing business with a company. 71% of the customers believe that a quick response from the customer service agents improves their experience drastically.

Let’s suppose you make every effort and investment to provide the best service to your customers. You create a well-developed customer care system, hire staff, make a team of customer service representatives, and train them to give a world-class service. But how do you know that your customer service agents are doing their job perfectly and your customers are satisfied with the service? The only way is to collect Customer Feedback.

Customer Feedback gives you valuable information about what your customers think and feel about the experience they get from your organization. Gathering feedback helps you to know whether your customers are satisfied with the service provided or not. Let’s learn why Customer Feedback about Customer Service agents is necessary to collect.

Why is Customer Feedback of Customer Service Agents Important?

Do your customer service agents respond on time? Are they welcoming and friendly with your customers? Are they doing their job the right way and providing the support your customers need? Are they able to improve Customer Experience? Feedback about the customer service agents provides the answer to all these questions.

To know more about the importance of collecting feedback about Customer Service Agents, let’s explore some of its advantages.

Benefits of Customer Feedback of Customer Service Agents

  1. It helps to measure Customer Satisfaction
  2. It helps to track the performance of the service agents
  3. It helps to retain customers and prevent churn
  4. It helps to improve your products and services
  5. It helps in developing effective processes

Let’s review how these benefits of Customer Feedback help you perform well in your business.

1. It helps to measure Customer Satisfaction
The biggest advantage of collecting Customer Feedback is that it is the key to measure Customer Satisfaction. When you collect feedback, you get to know whether and how much your customers are satisfied with the service your agents are providing them.

2. It helps to track the performance of the service agents
30% of customers consider communicating with a knowledgeable and friendly customer service agent the most crucial customer service aspect. Feedback about the Customer Service Agents helps you know how well they are doing their work. It enables you to see whether they can provide the needed support to the customers or not.

Customer Feedback helps you ensure that your agents respond to customers’ requests and concerns on time and do their best to satisfy them. It helps you track improvement in your agents' performances and know which agents need more training.

3. It helps to retain customers and prevent churn
Dissatisfied customers ultimately churn after a few instances. If the customers are unhappy with your services, Customer Feedback is the way to know about this. When you get to know about this dissatisfaction, you put in efforts to increase Customer Satisfaction and improve the experience.

When you put in your best efforts, take required actions on the negative feedback and inform the customers what you are doing for them, it changes their perception to a large extent. This helps to retain the customers and prevent churn. Studies suggest that increasing customer retention by 5% can increase your profits by 25% to 95%.

4. It helps to improve your products and services
Customer Feedback acts as a guideline to improve your products and services. It helps you identify the strengths and weaknesses of your products and services, which further helps empower the strengths and overcome the shortcomings and thus improve your products and services.

5. It helps in developing effective processes
Studies suggest that 83% of customers feel more loyal to those brands that respond to and resolve their issues. So the processes of Customer Service and support are vital to be effective.

Customer Feedback helps you identify the loopholes in these processes and work on them to develop a smooth process for the customers to raise requests and concerns and get support for them.

How to Collect and Use Actionable Customer Feedback for Service Agents

When you have learned about the advantages of collecting Customer Feedback about Customer Service Agents, the question arises as to how to collect feedback and use it most effectively so that proper actions can be taken on time and your business gets the maximum benefit of it. Let’s explore how to do it.

Ways to Collect and Use Customer Feedback about Customer Service Agents

  1. Use Customer Feedback Surveys to collect feedback
  2. Create tickets for issues
  3. Send Automated Customer Feedback Surveys
  4. Send Embedded surveys in Service Communication Emails
  5. Share Survey Results with Customer Service Agents

Let’s learn how to collect and use feedback for the benefit of your business.

1. Use Customer Feedback Surveys to collect feedback
To collect and use Customer Feedback, the first task is to ask your customers for feedback. The best way to ask customers for feedback is to create and send them Customer Feedback Surveys. You can use Customer Feedback Software for this purpose. A good Customer Feedback App and software allows you to create and send multiple types of surveys.

You can also use metric surveys, which are very effective in measuring Customer Satisfaction, loyalty, and the perceived efforts of the customers to get their work done. The popular metric surveys are:

a. NPS
NPS or Net Promoter Score is a popular metric that is used to measure Customer Loyalty in terms of the likeliness of the customers to refer a brand to their known ones. A simple question is asked to the customers.

“How likely are you to recommend us to your friends and colleagues?”

Here, a metric scale of 0 to 10 is provided to the customers to select a rating score where 10 means ‘Most Likely’ and 0 means ‘Not at all Likely’. As per the NPS Survey results, the respondents are divided into three categories: Promoters, Passives, and Detractors.

Promoters
are those who rate 9 and 10. They are considered the most satisfied and thus loyal customers and are most likely to recommend the brand to their known ones.

Passives are those that provide a rating of 7 or 8. They are usually satisfied customers but tend to try and switch to other brands as and when they get a better deal.

Detractors are those who rate between 0 to 6. They are considered dissatisfied customers and are most likely to switch to another brand and even share their bad experiences with their known ones, thus spreading bad word of mouth about the brand.

Net Promoter Score is measured by subtracting the percentage of Detractors from that of Promoters. It can lie anywhere between -100 to 100. A positive NPS is generally considered good and a negative NPS as bad.

b. CSAT Score
CSAT or Customer Satisfaction Score measures the level of Customer Satisfaction derived by the customers with their experience with your products, services, and the organization. The interaction with your Customer Service Agents primarily affects the level of Customer Satisfaction and experience. So it is helpful to measure CSAT to know how happy your customers are with your Customer Service Agents. A simple question is asked to the customers:

“How satisfied are you with our products and services?”

Five rating options are provided to the customers in different forms like self-explanatory smileys, star ratings, or adjectives like Excellent, Good, Average, Bad, and Poor.

The final score is calculated by finding the average score, which is done by dividing the total score by the number of respondents.

c. CES
CES or Customer Effort Score is a customer service metric that measures customers’ perceived efforts to get a job done or an issue resolved. This metric directly measures how well your Customer Service Agents work to help your customers and make their lives easier.

In a CES Survey, a simple question is asked to the customers:

“To what extent do you agree with the following statement: The Company made it easy for me to handle my issues.”

A seven rating scale is provided to the customers with the options as Strongly Disagree, Somewhat Disagree, Neither Agree Nor Disagree, Somewhat Agree, and Strongly Agree.

Results from 1-3 are considered negative, whereas responses from 5-7 are considered positive.

With effective Survey Software, you can also create and modify other types of surveys as per your industry and requirements like Customer Feedback Surveys, Patient Feedback Surveys, Student Feedback Surveys, Employee Feedback Surveys, Guest Feedback surveys, etc. You can send these surveys through multiple channels like email, SMS, website, QR codes, Android tablets, and smartphones.

2. Create tickets for issues
You can also choose to create Zendesk issues with the feedback provided by the customers. Suppose a customer provides negative feedback that involves a concern with the products and services. With the integration of survey software with software like Zendesk, Helpscout, and Freshdesk, you can choose issues and tickets to be created automatically with negative feedback raising the concern.

This will help you to use Customer Feedback effectively to take the appropriate actions required to improve customers' experience at the right time and Close the Feedback Loop.

3. Send Automated Customer Feedback Surveys
For getting information about the exact Customer Experience with your Customer Service Agents and the support process, you can use automation to send the feedback surveys at the right time. You can trigger surveys along with the ticket raised for the customers’ issues.

You can trigger surveys at events like raising a ticket, updating a ticket, and most appropriately closing a ticket to know the Customer Experience of getting an issue resolved.

4. Send Embedded surveys in Service Communication Emails
While providing support and services to the clients, there are many instances where email communication is carried out. In such cases, you can add embedded surveys in the body of the service communication emails. Email surveys are more likely to be responded to as they reach the customers as a part of the regular communication emails.

It is very simple to embed a Customer Feedback Survey in an email. You just need to copy the HTML code of the survey from your survey application and paste it into Zendesk’s email. It will pick the survey and display it embedded in the email body itself. These surveys are very beneficial to gauge the Customer Experience with the service agents at each touchpoint. They let you timely know if there is any concern between the process so you can take action at the right time before things worsen.

5. Share Survey Results with Customer Service Agents
When you have sent the feedback survey, and the customers have responded to it, the next step is to take action on the feedback received. 52% of the global customers feel that companies need to take Customer Feedback seriously and act on it. Did you know that 77% of the companies do not have well-developed systems for responding to feedback? And it’s not at all a wise decision to be one of them, right!

So you should develop an efficient process of responding to Customer Feedback, taking actions, and Closing the Feedback Loop effectively. You must train your service agents to take action on Customer Feedback and improve the experience. For this, it is essential to share the customers’ feedback with your agent, so they know how they perform and where their performance dissatisfied the customers.

You should use the sharing option for this purpose, especially if your feedback-gathering team and customer service agents are different. This feature gives you options to share the rating score given by the customers and their comments. You can choose to share either of them or both of them with the agents so that they know where things go wrong, what action they should take, and how they can avoid issues in the future.

This will help in improving both, your agents’ performances well as Customer Experience.

Conclusion
Customer Service Agents are the face of your company. It is evident to keep a watch on their performance and the way they deal with your customers. Customer Feedback is the best source of this information, and it is crucial to collect feedback about your service agents.

Zonka Feedback is a comprehensive Customer Feedback Software that you can use to create and send surveys to gather feedback from your customers about the services they get from your agents. With Zonka Feedback, you can create any type of survey and send them through multiple channels and ways.

Items.Title: Jak czytać 文档 | Jak czytać dokumentację
Items.Description:
我bardzo dobrze！

聂罗祖米兹？ 怀尼基

波德苏莫瓦尼

Czytanie dokumentacji jest istotną umiejętnością, której można się nauczyć tylko przez praktykę. Czytanie dokumentacji jest istotną umiejętnością, której można się nauczyć tylko przez praktykę。 Nie zniechęcaj się, jeśli na początku będzie Ci szło opornie; Nie zniechęcaj się, jeśli na początku będzie Ci szło opornie; z czasem będziesz sobie radził coraz lepiej. z czasem będziesz sobie radził coraz lepiej。

Chcesz wiedzieć więcej? Chcesz wiedzieć więcej?

Przeczytaj o tym, jak zajść daleko, stawiając małe kroki . Przeczytaj o tym、 jak zajść daleko、stawiając małe kroki 。



原文:

Jeśli zadajesz pytania, na które odpowiedzi można łatwo znaleźć w dokumentacji, reakcje będą zależeć od atmosfery miejsca pracy, nastawienia Twojego zespołu lub od forum, na którym piszesz. Osoby przyjaźnie nastawione odeślą Cię do źródeł. Natomiast osoby nastawione „mniej” przyjaźnie potraktują Cię skrótowcem RTFM – „przeczytaj tę ekhem przyjazną instrukcję” (ang. Read The Friendly Manual) – albo prześlą Ci link do Let Me Google That For You.

Lepiej nie dopuszczać do takich sytuacji.

Zobaczmy zatem, jak rozwinąć umiejętność czytania dokumentacji.

Otwórz ją

Najważniejsza sprawa – jeśli masz jakiekolwiek trudności w pracy, zacznij od próby znalezienia odpowiedzi w dokumentacji. Największą zaletą takiego podejścia jest skuteczność: w wielu przypadkach w ten sposób znajdziesz potrzebne informacje. Musisz oddać się lekturze i nieco poszperać. Im dalej będziesz na swojej ścieżce zawodowej, tym więcej problemów będzie stawać Ci na drodze – nie ma gotowego przewodnika po wszystkich niuansach programowania.

Masz jakiś problem? I bardzo dobrze!

Większość dokumentacji jest zwykle rzeczowa do bólu, a ich zadaniem jest udzielenie odpowiedzi na ewentualne pytania – nie jest to porywająca lektura. Dużo łatwiej skupić się na niej, kiedy szukasz rozwiązania problemu.

Nie cierpię czytać dokumentacji tylko po to, żeby rozeznać się w projekcie. Nie ma znaczenia, czy chodzi o nowy projekt, czy o nieznaną mi bibliotekę. Mam wrażenie, że to strata czasu, bo nie mam kontekstu, na którym mógłbym się oprzeć, i trudno jest zrozumieć powiązania między rzeczami, o których czytam.

Wolę zrobić coś po swojemu, potknąć się i wrócić do dokumentacji z konkretnym pytaniem. W takim wypadku przynajmniej wiem, czego nie wiem, i jestem zmotywowany do uzupełnienia braków w wiedzy.

Skorzystaj z funkcji wyszukiwania

Aby szybko uzyskać informacje na temat tego, jak rozwiązać problem, na który się natknąłeś, skorzystaj z narzędzi do wyszukiwania.

W przypadku bibliotek zewnętrznych możesz wykorzystać wyszukiwarki, dzięki czemu ograniczysz wyszukiwanie do strony internetowej projektu: po prostu dodaj do wyszukiwanej frazy site:<domain>:

Funkcja ta działa w Google, Bing i DuckDuckGo.

W dokumentacji znajdującej się w kodzie możesz zastosować te same narzędzia, z których korzystasz do przeszukiwania bazy kodu:

  • wyszukiwanie w edytorze,
  • ripgrep,
  • git grep.

Przykład na podstawie git grep:

$ git grep 'http\.post'
src/auto/injector.js: * $http.post(trackingUrl, trackedEvents);
src/ng/http.js: * $http.post('/someUrl', data, config).then(successCallback, errorCallback);
src/ng/http.js: * - {@link ng.$http#post $http.post}

Ręczne przeszukiwanie dokumentacji ma kilka zalet:

  • możesz zapoznać się z jej strukturą,
  • znajdziesz wszystkie fragmenty dokumentacji dotyczące Twojego problemu.

Dokumentacja API – suchy opis metod

Samouczek lub przewodnik dają większy kontekst.

Nie rozumiesz? Nic nie szkodzi!

Dokumentacja dokumentacji nierówna. Często nie są one łatwe w odbiorze. Ich twórcy mogą zakładać, że czytelnik posiada już pewną wiedzę o systemie, i nie wszystko będzie wprost opisane. Przez takie sytuacje nowi członkowie zespołu mogą czuć się zbici z tropu.

Najbardziej problematyczne okazują się projekty komercyjne closed source. Jeśli za utrzymywanie bazy kodu i jej dokumentacji odpowiada niewielki zespół, mogą minąć miesiące, zanim przeczyta go nowa para oczu, próbując rozeznać się w tym wszystkim.

Projekty open source są zwykle „łatwiejsze w obsłudze”. Te bez dobrej dokumentacji mają mniejsze szanse na sukces.

Dobry przewodnik nie jest zły

Jeśli w projekcie dostępny jest samouczek albo przewodnik, dobrze jest z niego skorzystać. Jeśli często borykasz się z podobnym problemem, znajdź dotyczący go fragment i po prostu go przeczytaj. Dobrze zrobiony przewodnik da Ci kontekst potrzebny do pełnego zrozumienia wykorzystywanego mechanizmu.

Zanurz się w odpowiedniej części API

Opis API to najbardziej techniczna z dokumentacji. Jest zwykle rzeczowa i brakuje jej opisów kontekstów, w których możesz zastosować konkretną metodę. Przedstawia wszystkie klasy, metody, argumenty i dane wyjściowe będące na Twoim podorędziu.

Jeżeli opis ten jest dostępny, zapoznaj się z różnymi fragmentami dotyczącymi tych samych lub podobnych rzeczy. Często jest tak, że rozwiązanie polega na odesłaniu do innej metody – więc jeśli nadal nic nie wiesz po przeczytaniu jakiegoś fragmentu, sprawdź, czy opis innych metod nie będzie bardziej przydatny.

Wyniki

W przypadku metod będziesz spotykał się albo ze zmianą stanu wewnętrznego obiektu, albo z danymi wyjściowymi. Przewodnik po API powinien dobrze opisywać oba te przypadki.

Zmiana danych wejściowych

Często zdarza się, że metody akceptują dane wejściowe w wielu kombinacjach argumentów. Dokumentacja opisze Ci pełen wachlarz możliwości wprowadzenia wszystkiego, czego potrzebujesz.

Podsumowanie

Czytanie dokumentacji jest istotną umiejętnością, której można się nauczyć tylko przez praktykę. Nie zniechęcaj się, jeśli na początku będzie Ci szło opornie; z czasem będziesz sobie radził coraz lepiej.

Chcesz wiedzieć więcej?

Przeczytaj o tym, jak zajść daleko, stawiając małe kroki.

Items.Title: 使用 D3.js 的条形图 | Bar Chart using D3.js
Items.Description:
沙巴贝吉

发表于如果你使用 npm，npm install d3。 您还可以在 GitHub 上下载最新版本。 对于现代浏览器中的普通 HTML，从 Skypack 导入 D3： 

 <script type= "module" > import * as d3 from " https://cdn.skypack.dev/d3@7 " ; const div = d3 . selectAll ( " div " ); </script>

对于遗留环境，您可以从基于 npm 的 CDN（例如 jsDelivr）加载 D3 的 UMD 包； 导出 d3 全局：

 <script src= "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/d3@7" ></script> <script> const div = d3 . selectAll ( " div " ); </script>

您还可以使用独立的 D3 微库。 例如，d3-selection：

 <script type= "module" > import { selectAll } from " https://cdn.skypack.dev/d3-selection@3 " ; const div = selectAll ( " div " ); </script>

首先使用 namse 创建 3 个文件夹：index.html、index.css、index.js 然后打开 index.html 并编写以下代码：

 <html> <head> <link rel= "stylesheet" href= "index.css" > <title> Learn D3.js </title> </head> <body> <h1> Bar Chart using D3.js </h1> <svg class= "bar-chart" ></svg> <script src= "https://d3js.org/d3.v4.min.js" ></script> <script src= "index.js" ></script> </body> </html>

然后打开 index.css 并复制以下代码：

 .bar-chart { background-color : #C7D9D9 ; } .bar { fill : #115D8C ; }

最后将这段代码复制到 index.js 中：

 // javascript var dataset = [ 80 , 100 , 56 , 120 , 180 , 30 , 40 , 120 , 160 ]; var svgWidth = 500 , svgHeight = 300 , barPadding = 5 ; var barWidth = ( svgWidth / dataset . length ); var svg = d3 . select ( ' svg ' ) . attr ( " width " , svgWidth ) . attr ( " height " , svgHeight ); var barChart = svg . selectAll ( " rect " ) . data ( dataset ) . enter () . append ( " rect " ) . attr ( " y " , function ( d ) { return svgHeight - d }) . attr ( " height " , function ( d ) { return d ; }) . attr ( " width " , barWidth - barPadding ) . attr ( " transform " , function ( d , i ) { var translate = [ barWidth * i , 0 ]; return " translate( " + translate + " ) " ; });

结果将是这样的：

卡文杜桑图萨 - Apr 18 4月18日



原文:

Saba beigi

Posted on May 18

D3 (or D3.js) is a JavaScript library for visualizing data using web standards. D3 helps you bring data to life using SVG, Canvas and HTML. D3 combines powerful visualization and interaction techniques with a data-driven approach to DOM manipulation, giving you the full capabilities of modern browsers and the freedom to design the right visual interface for your data. f you use npm, npm install d3. You can also download the latest release on GitHub. For vanilla HTML in modern browsers, import D3 from Skypack:

<script type="module"> import * as d3 from "https://cdn.skypack.dev/d3@7"; const div = d3.selectAll("div"); </script>

For legacy environments, you can load D3’s UMD bundle from an npm-based CDN such as jsDelivr; a d3 global is exported:

<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/d3@7"></script>
<script> const div = d3.selectAll("div"); </script>

You can also use the standalone D3 microlibraries. For example, d3-selection:

<script type="module"> import {selectAll} from "https://cdn.skypack.dev/d3-selection@3"; const div = selectAll("div"); </script>

first create 3 folder with namse: index.html, index.css,index.js then open index.html and write this codes:

<html> <head> <link rel="stylesheet" href="index.css"> <title>Learn D3.js</title> </head> <body> <h1>Bar Chart using D3.js</h1> <svg class="bar-chart"></svg> <script src="https://d3js.org/d3.v4.min.js"></script> <script src="index.js"></script> </body>
</html>

then open index.css and copy this codes:

.bar-chart { background-color: #C7D9D9; } .bar { fill: #115D8C; }

and finally copy this codes in index.js:

 // javascript
var dataset = [80, 100, 56, 120, 180, 30, 40, 120, 160]; var svgWidth = 500, svgHeight = 300, barPadding = 5;
var barWidth = (svgWidth / dataset.length); var svg = d3.select('svg') .attr("width", svgWidth) .attr("height", svgHeight); var barChart = svg.selectAll("rect") .data(dataset) .enter() .append("rect") .attr("y", function(d) { return svgHeight - d }) .attr("height", function(d) { return d; }) .attr("width", barWidth - barPadding) .attr("transform", function (d, i) { var translate = [barWidth * i, 0]; return "translate("+ translate +")"; });

the result will be this:

Kavindu Santhusa - Apr 18
Items.Author: sababg

Items.Title: GitLive 现在可以与 WebStorm 中的任何 Git 存储库一起使用！ | GitLive Now Works With Any Git Repository in WebStorm!
Items.Description:
我们很高兴地宣布 GitLive 现在可以在离线模式下开箱即用，与 WebStorm 中的任何 Git 存储库一起使用！

以前我们只支持来自四大托管服务（GitHub、GitLab、Bitbucket、Azure DevOps）的存储库，并要求您在开始使用 GitLive 之前授权访问该服务。

现在，当您安装 GitLive 时，它​​将以离线模式启动。 离线模式仅使用本地 Git 存储库中的数据，不会将任何内容发送到您的网络之外。 您的存储库应该有一个远程，但它可以托管在任何地方。

在 GitLive 工具窗口中，您将看到当前在默认分支之前的所有分支作者，对分支的最后一次提交决定了它将出现在谁之下。

深入到单个作者的分支以查看更改的文件并检查差异以及查看相关的问题或拉取请求。

它甚至会在更改与您自己的更改发生冲突时向您显示 - 一种合并冲突的预警系统！

单击作者的头像以检查更改并查看差异，它们来自哪个分支，甚至樱桃选择直接更改到您的本地文件中。

而且您仍然可以实时登录并在团队窗口中查看您的队友未推送的更改，在您进行冲突更改时在装订线中收到通知，以及同时拨打电话和代码共享以一起编辑。

登录目前仍需要来自四大托管服务的存储库，但我们将很快添加对其他服务的支持，包括自托管！

还没有安装 GitLive 插件？ 您可以在此处找到它，如果想了解有关最新版本的更多信息，请查看此博客文章。



原文:

We are happy to announce GitLive now works, out-of-the-box in offline mode, with any Git repository in WebStorm!

Previously we only supported repositories from the big four hosting services (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, Azure DevOps) and required you to authorize access to the service before you could start using GitLive.

Now when you install GitLive it will start in offline mode. The offline mode uses the data from your local Git repository only and nothing is sent outside your network. Your repository should have a remote but it can be hosted anywhere.

In the GitLive tool window you'll see all the authors of branches currently ahead of your default branch, the last commit to a branch determines who it will appear under.

Drill down into an individual author’s branches to see the files changed and inspect diffs plus view the associated issue or pull request.

The changes from the branches shown in the GitLive tool window also appear for your current file in the gutter of your editor. The gutter indicators show the differences in the file from other branches compared to your local version. It even shows you when the changes conflict with your own - a kind of early warning system for merge conflicts!

Click on the author's avatar to inspect a change and see the diff, what branch they're from and even cherry-pick changes straight into your local file.

And you can still sign in to go real-time and see your teammate's unpushed changes in the team window, get notified in the gutter the moment you make a conflicting change, plus make calls and codeshare to edit together simultaneously.

Signing in still requires a repository from the big four hosting services for now but we'll be adding support for other services, including self-hosted, soon!

Don't have the GitLive plugin installed yet? You can find it here, and if want to learn more about the latest release, check out this blog post.
Items.Author: andrewgl

Items.Title: AWS 积分：如何为您的初创公司获取免费积分？ | AWS Credits: How To Obtain Free Credits for Your Startup?
Items.Description:
阿尔戈英雄

发表于作为一家新成立的科技初创公司，您决定使用 AWS 服务，下一个问题是：相关成本是多少？ 我如何在不预先承担额外成本的情况下为我的客户提供可靠的产品？ 正是在这种情况下，像您这样的初创公司需要利用免费的 AWS 积分。

它现在已经成为世界各地初创公司的事实，以其有前途的和广泛的功能。 什么是 AWS 积分？

积分可以直接从 AWS 的账单页面兑换，并一直使用到用完或过期。

获得免费积分的 5 种方法

  1. 提供给创业公司自筹资金或自力更生的个人创始人。 你可以在这里申请。
  2. 拥有最多 A 轮融资的初创公司可以申请此类别。 此激活等级提供高达 100,000 美元的积分和 10,000 美元的 AWS 业务支持积分。 如果此级别适合您，您可以在此处申请。 如果您还没有加入任何加速器，您可以考虑申请著名的创业加速器计划，例如YC和Techstars 。 或者，您可以尝试联系您所在城市的本地创业孵化器，看看他们是否有合适的项目。 是的，所有这些还附带 AWS 积分和许多其他特权。 使用slidebean或tracxn 之类的工具来找到合适的程序。
  3. 其中包括免费试用、12 个月免费和永久免费选项。 此类服务通常在计算时间、存储或速度方面存在使用限制。 您可以了解有关免费套餐的更多信息，看看它是否符合您的项目要求。
  4. 这对于那些仍在弄清楚 AWS 如何使他们的项目和组织受益的人很有用。 有兴趣的人可以看这里。
  5. 成为社区的一员不仅可以获得知识，还可以获得可用于在 AWS 上部署应用程序的积分。 在此处查看AWS 事件的完整列表。

还有一些与您最喜欢的提供 AWS 积分的组织相关的交易。 请随意检查此处列出的内容。

对于来自印度的读者：

从免费积分中受益的更多方式

除了向初创公司提供免费信用外，还有其他几个信用池分配给其他类型的组织和项目。 他们之中有一些是：

使用 AWS 积分时要记住的事项

  • 企业可以获得最高 100,000 美元的 AWS 积分的生命周期价值。 此外，在特定情况下，具有 ML 工作负载的合格公司可以获得高达 250,000 美元的奖金。
  • 所有发行的信用都有到期日，通常为 1 年或 2 年。 确保您能够在积分到期前充分利用积分
  • 积分仅适用于收到积分的帐户。 可以从计费控制台的首选项页面打开或关闭信用共享
  • 积分分为两层：开始层（5000 美元）、加速层（10,000 美元 - 20,000 美元及更多）。 申请学分时，请选择 Start 层级，如果您发现需要更多学分，请再次申请
  • 它们通常与账户或组织相关联，并且只能用于定义的 AWS 服务
  • 信用申请需要不同的时间来批准，从几天到一个月不等，请提前计划在需要时获得信用。

准备发货？

现在您的 AWS 账户中已准备好项目并获得免费积分，是时候将您的项目部署到生产环境了。 我的公司 Argonaut 提供了一个易于使用的工具来自动化您的部署。 无论您是在 AWS Lambda Serverless 上部署还是使用 Kubernetes，Argonaut 都会负责连接您的存储库、管理环境和 AWS 资源，并使整个部署过程无缝衔接。 小型团队永远免费，现在就注册并在Argonaut尝试一下。



原文:

Argonaut

Posted on May 18

As a newly established tech startup, you’ve decided to use AWS services, the next question is: What are the costs associated? And how am I going to provide a reliable product for my customers without taking on excess costs upfront? It is precisely in these kinds of situations where startups like yours need to leverage free AWS credits.

AWS has over 1,000,000 active user accounts. It has now become the defacto for startups across the world with its promising and wide array of features. AWS Credits can give your startup initial savings on cloud costs so that you can use that capital to boost other initiatives such as shipping the product, hiring the right team, focusing on sales pipelines, Ad spending, etc.

What are AWS credits?

AWS credits are promotional coupons offered by Amazon Web Services (AWS) in order to allow new customers to use their AWS services for a certain amount of time. Credits can be redeemed straight from AWS’s billing page and are applied until they are exhausted or expired.

5 ways to obtain your free credits

  1. AWS Activate Founders - This is the first place to look for credits. Provided to individual founders whose startups are self-funded or bootstrapped. AWS activate is amazon’s program to support budding entrepreneurs. Activate Founders usually provides $1000 in credits with $350 in developer support credits and other offers. You can apply here.
  2. AWS Activate Portfolio - For funded or un-funded startups that are associated with an incubator, accelerator, venture-capital firm, or other organization that is an AWS Activate Provider. Startups with up to series A funding can apply under this category. This activate tier provides up to $100,000 in credits and $10,000 in AWS Business support credits. If this tier is right for you, you can apply here. If you aren’t yet part of any accelerator, you may consider applying to renowned startup accelerator programs like YC and Techstars. Alternatively, you can try approaching a local startup incubator in your city and see if they have any suitable programs. Yes, all these also come with AWS credits and a bunch of other perks. Use tools like slidebean or tracxn to find a suitable program.
  3. AWS Free Tier - There is also a free tier available to use AWS. These include free trials, 12-month free, and forever free options. Such services often come with usage limits in terms of compute time, storage or speed. You can find out more about the Free Tier to see if it fits your project requirements.
  4. AWS Proof Of Concept Program - This program provides organizations with $1000 in credits and an architecture review session for free. This is useful for those still figuring out how AWS might benefit their project and organization. Interested people can check it out here.
  5. AWS Webinars & Events - Attending AWS webinars and events is another way to get access to more free credits. Being part of the community can be rewarding not only in terms of knowledge but also with credits that you can use to deploy your apps on AWS. View a full list of AWS events here.

The ultimate list of AWS free credit deals

There are also several deals associated with your favorite organizations that offer AWS credits. Feel free to check the ones listed here.

For our readers from India:

More ways to benefit from free credits

In addition to providing free credits to startups, there are several other credit pools allotted to other types of organizations and projects. Some of them are:

Things to remember when using AWS credits

  • A business can receive a maximum lifetime value of $100,000 in AWS credits. Furthermore, qualifying companies with ML workloads can get up to $250,000 in specific cases.
  • All credits issued have expiration dates, commonly 1 or 2 years. Ensure you are able to make the most out of your credits before they expire
  • Credits are applied to only the account that received the credits. Credit sharing can be turned on or off from the billing console’s preferences page
  • Credits come in two tiers: Start tier ($5000), Accelerate tier ($10,000 - $20,000 and more). When applying for credits, go for the Start tier, and then request again if you see the need for more credits
  • AWS credits do not have any cash value and are not redeemable for cash
  • They are usually linked to an account or organization and can be used for defined AWS services only
  • Credit applications take different amounts of time to approve ranging from a few days to a month, plan in advance to have the credits when you need them.

Ready to ship?

Now that you have your project ready and free credits in your AWS account, it’s time to deploy your project to production. My company Argonaut offers an easy-to-use tool to automate your deployments. Whether you’re deploying on AWS Lambda Serverless or using Kubernetes, Argonaut takes care of connecting your repo, managing environments, and AWS resources, and making the entire deployment process seamless. It’s forever free for small teams, sign up now and give it a try at Argonaut.

Items.Title: 为什么 Android 应用程序测试很重要？ | Why Android Application Testing Is Important?
Items.Description:
移动时代已经开始，并且在任何先进设备取代我们日常活动的方式之前，它仍然会占据主导地位。 移动应用程序正变得更加成熟、高效，应用程序开发公司正专注于开发企业级移动应用程序，消费者可以使用这些应用程序执行日常活动。 这场革命正是由谷歌制造的开源智能手机操作系统 Android 带来的。 截至 2018 年第二季度，基于 Android 的智能手机占市场总使用份额的近 88%。 由于错误和缓慢的应用程序没有用处，最终用户在卸载它们之前不会三思而后行。 在本文中，我们将讨论为什么应用程序开发人员在将其部署到生产环境之前正确测试他们的 Android 应用程序很重要。

嘿，你知道二维码生成器吗 - 生成随机二维码的免费在线工具。 测试应用程序的可用性

可用性测试可能是决定客户如何喜欢您的应用程序的最重要的测试。

  • 在功能之前，消费者会检查您的应用程序的外观。 具有动态按钮、渐变和颜色的有吸引力的应用程序将比具有默认按钮和颜色样式的平淡无奇的应用程序更具吸引力。

  • 如果您的应用程序是为学生或纳税人等特定受众群体设计的，那么从用户的角度进行测试思考很重要。 用户只有在满足他们的需求时才会安装和使用您的应用程序。

  • 产品应该易于使用。 对于已经精通技术的开发人员和测试人员来说，使用该应用程序非常容易。 但是当涉及到用户时，应该进行测试以确保应用程序不那么复杂且易于使用。

希望在 Real Device Cloud 上执行 Android 和 iOS 应用程序测试，请查看下面的视频 -

应用程序的性能测试

使用 Android 智能手机的人几乎 90% 的时间都花在了应用程序上。 在开发应用程序时，应执行性能测试以确保以下因素。

  • 该应用程序应该得到很好的优化。 在卸载需要大量时间来加载和执行功能的慢速应用程序之前，消费者不会三思而后行。

  • 如果应用程序连接到互联网，则应执行负载测试以检查当多个用户尝试访问它时它如何响应。

  • 应在网络覆盖率低的情况下检查响应时间。

  • 在处理多个数据时，经常会观察到应用程序崩溃。 应进行性能测试以检查是否不会发生这种情况。

测试应用程序的跨浏览器兼容性

通常，应用程序可能需要在浏览器上打开一个外部窗口。 例如，涉及支付网关或重定向到其他应用程序的应用程序。 对于这些部分，跨浏览器测试非常重要。

  • 应检查应在浏览器上运行的应用程序部分是否在 Android 设备的本地浏览器上正常工作。

  • 应执行测试以确保它在所有主要使用的 Android 设备浏览器中正常运行。

  • 手动测试跨浏览器兼容性设备会消耗大量时间。 在这种情况下，基于云的测试平台是您问题的解决方案。 牢记这一点，除了现有的移动模拟器和模拟器之外，LambdaTest 很快就会推出原生移动应用程序测试。 您可以使用他们的 iOS 和 android 模拟器软件测试您的网站和 Web 应用程序的跨浏览器兼容性。

测试您的应用程序的安全性

在开发银行应用程序或处理汇款的应用程序时，应用程序的安全性非常重要。 应进行相应的测试以检查

  • 存储或 cookie 和缓存被禁用。 注销后，当按下返回按钮时，用户应该会发现会话已过期，而不是返回应用程序界面。

  • 应用程序应正确验证用户的身份验证。 除了用户名和密码，如果指纹等生物特征安全存在，它应该可以正常工作。

  • 对于在线交易，应执行自动化测试脚本以检测任何欺诈活动。

设备内存使用情况

通常会看到应用程序使用大量物理内存和内部存储来由设备处理，从而导致大多数用户不安装应用程序

  • 如果应用程序使用本地设备存储照片和视频等数据，则应进行测试以确保它不会占用太多空间。 一种优选的方法是通过云处理数据存储。

  • 如果应用程序针对特定区域或用户组，则应针对该区域中最常用的设备进行研究。 应执行适当的Android 设备测试，以确保它不会使用过多的 RAM，从而导致频繁的应用程序崩溃和设备挂起。

执行操作时的中断

假设您正在使用银行应用程序，但由于收到消息或呼叫而突然停止工作。 应进行适当的测试以检查您的应用程序如何处理第三方中断。

  • 检查它如何处理来电和消息，它们应该在后台运行，而不是根据所执行功能的重要性最小化应用程序。

  • 应用程序通知应该是微妙的，并且不要太频繁而激怒用户。

  • 假设用户在使用应用程序时需要接听电话或执行一些其他操作。 应进行测试以检查应用程序在后台如何处理正在进行的过程。

您检查过 HTML 到 Markdown 转换器吗？ HTML（超文本标记语言）是一种标准方式，用于标记 Internet 上的内容，以便它在 Web 浏览器中正确显示。 使用最新技术保持更新

今天开发的大多数应用程序都与智能手表、智能眼镜等第三方设备集成，或者开发的应用程序通常具有增强现实等功能

  • 应进行设备测试以检查与智能设备和 android 应用程序的数据集成的处理情况。 应用程序和智能设备应该能够正常通信而不会出现任何中断。

  • 对于处理增强现实的应用程序，适用于许多照片和视频应用程序，应检查响应时间。 测试人员还应该检查应用程序的效率和可用性。

使用移动应用程序时遇到的大多数问题不是在开发过程中遇到的，而是在真实设备中测试应用程序时遇到的。 这就是为什么应将Android 应用程序测试视为应用程序开发生命周期中的一个重要阶段，以确保您的应用程序提高 PlayStore 评级并保证在功能和质量保证方面的成功。



原文:

The age of the mobile has already started and it’s still going to rule until any advanced device replace the means of performing our day to day activities. Mobile apps are becoming more matured, efficient and app development companies are focusing on developing enterprise grade mobile applications using which consumers can perform their day to day activities. This revolution was brought by none other than Android, the open source smartphone operating system manufactured by Google. Till the 2nd quarter of 2018, Android based smartphones cover almost 88% of the total usage share in the market. Since buggy and slow apps have no use and end users will not think twice before uninstalling them. In this article, let’s discuss why it is important for application developers to properly test their android applications before deploying them into production.

Hey,do you know about QR Code Generator — Free online tool to generate random QR codes. QR code generators make it easy to store data on a QR code, such as text or website addresses.

Testing the Usability of Your Application

Usability testing is perhaps the most important testing that decides how customers will like your application.

  • Before functionality, consumers check how your application looks like. An attractive app with dynamic buttons, gradients and colors will be more attractive than a bland looking app with default button and color styles.

  • If your application is designed for a specific group of audience like students or taxpayers, it is important to conduct the testing thinking from the perspective of a user. A user will only install and use your app if it meets their needs.

  • The product should be easy to use. For developers and testers who are already tech savvy, using the application is very easy. But when it comes to the users, testing should be conducted to ensure that the application is less complex and easy to use.

Looking to perform Android and iOS app testing on Real Device Cloud, check out our video below –

Performance Testing of Your Application

Almost 90% time of a person using an Android smartphone is spent on apps. While developing an application, performance testing should be executed to ensure the following factors.

  • The app should be well optimized. Consumers will not think twice before uninstalling a slow app that takes a lot of time to load and perform a function.

  • If the app is connected to the internet, load testing should be executed to check how it responds when several users try to access it.

  • Response time should be checked under low network coverage.

  • While handling multiple data an app is often observed to crash. Performance testing should be carried out to check this does not happen.

Testing Cross Browser Compatibility of Your Application

Often, an application may require to open an external window at the browser. For example, applications involving payment gateways or redirecting to other applications. For those sections, cross browser testing is highly important.

  • It should be checked whether the section of the application that is supposed to run at a browser is working properly at the local browser of the Android device.

  • Testing should be executed to ensure that it runs properly in all major used browsers of Android devices.

  • Manually testing cross browser compatibility device wise consumes a lot of time. In that case, cloud-based testing platforms are the solution to your problem. Keeping that in mind LambdaTest is soon coming up with native mobile application testing in addition to already existing mobile emulators and simulators. You can test your website and web applications for cross browser compatibility using their iOS and android emulator software.

Testing How Secure Your App Is

While developing a banking app or an app that deals with money transfer, security of your app is very important. Testing should be carried out accordingly to check

  • Storage or cookies and cache is disabled. After logging out, when the back button is pressed, user should find the session expired instead of going back to the app interface.

  • The app should properly verify the authentication of the user. Apart from username and password, if biometric security like fingerprint is there, it should work properly.

  • For online transactions, automated testing scripts should be executed to detect any fraudulent activities.

Memory Usage of the Device

Often an app is seen to use lot of physical memory and internal storage to be handled by a device, leading to maximum users not installing the app

  • If the app uses local device for data storage like photos and videos, testing should be carried out to ensure that it does not take too much space. A preferable method is to handle the data storage via cloud.

  • If the app is targeted for a specific zone or user group, research should be carried out to target the most used devices in that zone. Proper Android device tests should be executed to ensure that it does not use too much RAM, leading to frequent application crashing and device hanging.

Disruptions While Performing an Operation

Let’s suppose you are using a banking application and it stops working suddenly because of an incoming message or call. Proper testing should be carried out to check how your application handles third party disruptions.

  • Check how it handles incoming calls and messages, they should be running in background instead of minimizing the app depending on the importance of a functionality performed.

  • App notifications should be subtle and not too much frequent to irritate the user.

  • Let’s suppose the user needs to receive a call or perform some other operations while using the app. Testing should be carried out to check how the ongoing process is handled by the app in background.

Did you check out HTML to Markdown Converter — HTML (the Hypertext Markup Language) is a standard way for content on the Internet to be marked up so that it displays properly in a web browser. A Markdown file is a plain text file that uses formatting symbols to make text bold, define headers and indentation, or create lists.

Keeping it Updated with Latest Technologies

Most of the apps developed today are integrated with third party devices like smartwatches, smartglasses or often apps are developed with features like augmented reality

  • Device testing should be carried out to check how well data integration with the smart device and the android application is handled. The application and the smart device should be able to communicate properly without any disruption.

  • For apps that deal with augmented reality, applicable for many photo and video apps, response time should be checked. The tester should also check the efficiency and usability of the app.

Majority of the problems faced while using a mobile application are faced not during development, but while testing the app in a real device. This is the reason for which Android Application testing should be considered to be an important phase in the app development life cycle to ensure that your application increases the PlayStore rating and guarantees success in terms of function as well as quality assurance.
Items.Author: arnabroychowdhury

Items.Title: 关于软件开发的最佳文章！ | Best articles on software development!
Items.Description:
最近我读了很多关于软件开发的文章，我忍不住分享一些我最近读到的好文章。 我希望你会像我一样喜欢阅读它们。 您可能无法一口气读完所有这些内容，但您可以将它们添加为书签，稍后再回来阅读。 我鼓励你至少读一遍。

它很长，有很多细节，所以请确保您有足够的时间阅读，或将其添加为书签以便以后阅读。 在这次采访中让我印象深刻的台词是：“几乎不言而喻，你永远不会真正找到问题的完美答案。你只会找到问题最少的答案。” 这是一篇很棒的文章，它详细讨论了 DI（依赖注入）和控制反转。 软件开发人员必须知道的两个最重要的概念。

有趣的阅​​读你会喜欢的，相信我！

我相信对于开发人员而言，了解有关 Unicode 和不同编码技术的基础知识非常重要。 这是一篇关于无处不在的 UTF-8 的非常容易理解的文章。

这篇文章是我读过的关于 Web 应用程序安全性的最佳文章之一。 首先它讨论了简单的验证和编码以避免许多注入攻击，然后讨论了通过安全 HTTP 保护传输中的数据（以避免中间人攻击）。

我会继续在这个列表中添加更多我觉得有趣和有见地的文章。 请在评论中分享您最喜欢的文章，我会在这里添加它们！

阿斯米特马拉卡纳瓦尔 - 马蒂厄·布兰迪诺 - May 12 5月12日



原文:

Recently I have been reading a lot about software development in general and I couldn't keep myself from sharing some of the good articles I read recently. I hope you will enjoy reading them as much as I did. You may not finish reading all of them in one sitting, but you can bookmark them and come back to them later. I encourage you to read them at least once.

https://www.artima.com/articles/james-gosling-on-java-may-2001 This is a very old interview taken by Bill Venners which you might find very enlightening. It's long with lots of details, so make sure you have enough time to read, or bookmark it to read it later. The lines that stood out for me from this interview: "It's almost a truism that you never actually find a perfect answer to a problem. You just find the answer that has the least problems." - James Gosling

"One of my favorite lines is that computers are driven by Moore's Law, which is an exponential process, and human beings are driven by Darwinism." - James Gosling

https://martinfowler.com/articles/injection.html
This is a great article which discusses in length about DI (dependency injection) and Inversion of Control. Two most important concepts a software developer MUST know.

https://www.joelonsoftware.com/2005/12/29/the-perils-of-javaschools-2/
Interesting read you will enjoy, trust me!

https://blog.hubspot.com/website/what-is-utf-8
I believe it’s very important for a developer to know basics about Unicode and different encoding techniques. This is a very easy to follow article on ubiquitous UTF-8.

https://martinfowler.com/articles/web-security-basics.html
This article is one of the best ones I read on web application security. First it talks about simple validation & encoding to avoid many injection attacks and then talks about securing data in transit (to avoid man-in-the-middle attack) through Secured HTTP.

I will keep adding to this list more articles I find interesting and insightful. Please share your favourite articles in the comments as well and I will add them up here!

Asmit Malakannawar - Apr 13

Matthieu Blandineau - May 12

Items.Title: 如何在 Angular Web 应用程序中实现滚动动画 - 使用 AOS 库 | HOW TO IMPLEMENT ANIMATE ON SCROLL IN ANGULAR WEB APPS - USING THE AOS LIBRARY
Items.Description:
介绍

动画激起用户对应用程序的兴趣。 不仅如此，考虑到在屏幕上进行戏剧性的过渡和移动可以在页面内容加载时保持用户注意力这一事实，动画还可以用于改善 UX（用户体验）。 在本文中，我们将介绍如何使用Animate On Scroll (AOS)库为 Angular 网页制作动画。

学习目标

在本文结束时，您将学会如何：

  • 安装和配置 Animate On Scroll Library
  • 在 Angular 应用程序中初始化网页并为其设置动画。

先决条件

要充分利用本教程，需要对以下内容有基本的了解；

  • 角
  • 打字稿

让我们开始逐步了解
达到本教程的学习目标。

在我们开始之前，必须运行一个 Angular 应用程序。 这可以通过执行以下命令序列来完成：

 ng new my-app // ? Would you like to add Angular routing? Yes // ? Which stylesheet format would you like to use? CSS

我们所有的路由配置都需要在 Angular 项目的 app-routing.module.ts 文件中定义。 如果我们对问题“您想添加 Angular 路由吗？”回答“是”，Angular CLI 会将 app-routing.module.ts 文件添加到我们的 Angular 项目中。

这会将我们的目录更改为 myApp 文件夹。

默认情况下，这将在http://localhost:4200上服务并打开我们的 Angular 项目。 这样我们现在可以查看我们的项目。

我们将在这一步安装和配置动画库，以便在项目中可以访问和使用它。 要安装库，请运行以下命令：

上述命令将安装动画库，一旦安装成功，重要的是更新 angular.json 文件中的样式数组以包含动画库。 为此，请打开 angular.json 文件并向其中添加以下行；

 “ node_modules / aos / dist / aos . css ”

正确完成后，我们已经成功安装并配置了 AOS 库，这使它可以在我们的项目中使用。

我们可能需要重新启动服务器，以便在安装后使用最近的更改更新我们的项目，但前提是我们的项目似乎已过时。

在这一步中，我们最终将使动画栩栩如生，并在我们滚动浏览网页时使它们工作。 让我们开始吧，看看会发生什么。
首先，我们必须打开我们想要的组件的TS文件，例如“home-component.ts”，导入AOS库，并对其进行初始化。 这可以通过以下概述的步骤来完成；

  1. 导入库：在所需的 component.ts 文件中，添加导入；
  1. 初始化功能：要使 AOS 库正常运行，调用 component.ts 文件的 ngOnInit 中的 init() 函数很重要。 这可以简单地通过添加以下代码行来完成：

通过这样做，AOS 库已初始化，我们的动画已准备好执行操作。 但是在我们看到效果之前，我们必须打开我们的component.html文件（例如home-component.html），它必须是我们刚刚处理的ts文件的html组件，并使用data-aos属性设置动画我们想要的 div。 例子;

 <div data-aos= "fade-up" data-aos-duration= "3000" > <!-- our contents —-> </div>

上面的代码会在滚动时为 div 添加淡入淡出的动画，但 AOS 库的功能不限于此。 要发现更多动画，Animate on Scroll 官方网站有库提供的动画和效果体验。 您可以在此处查看并注意当您向下和向上滚动页面时效果如何发生。

结论。

到目前为止，在本文中，我们已经能够看到使用 AOS 库在其页面上设置具有 Animate On Scroll 效果的 Angular 应用程序是多么容易。 随时欢迎在评论中提出问题和建议。 下一篇文章见。
快乐编码！

谢谢你读到这里。 我希望您发现本教程很有用。 如果您有任何问题或意见，请在评论部分留下。

克里斯蒂安·费尔南多—— May 3 5月3日

Muhammad Bilal Mohib-ul-Nabi - Muhammad Bilal Mohib-ul-Nabi - May 13 5月13日



原文:

Introduction

Animations pique a user's interest in applications. Not only that, animations can also be used to improve UX(user experience) considering the fact that making dramatic transitions and movements on the screen can be a way to retain a user’s attention while the content of a page loads . In this article, we’ll go over how to use the Animate On Scroll (AOS) library to animate Angular web pages.

Learning Objectives

At the end of this article, you would have learned how to:

  • Install and configure the Animate On Scroll Library
  • Initialize and animate web pages in Angular applications.

Prerequisites

To get the most out of this tutorial, a basic understanding of the following is required;

  • HTML
  • CSS
  • Angular
  • Typescript

Let's get started by going over the step-by-step process to
achieve the learning objectives of this tutorial.

1. Setting up/installing an Angular app.

An angular app must be running before we begin at all. This can be accomplished by executing the following sequence of commands:

ng new my-app

 // ? Would you like to add Angular routing? Yes
 // ? Which stylesheet format would you like to use? CSS

All of our routing configuration would need to be defined in our angular project's app-routing.module.ts file. Angular CLI will add the app-routing.module.ts file to our Angular project if we answer "YES" to the question "Would you like to add Angular routing?".

This would change our directory to myApp folder.

This would serve and open our Angular project on http://localhost:4200 by default. This way we can now view our project.

2. Configuring/installing the Animate On Scroll (AOS) library.

We will install and configure the animation library in this step so that it can be accessed and used in the project. To install the library, run the following command:

The above command will install the animation library, and once it has been successfully installed, it is important to update the styles array in the angular.json file to include the animation library. To do this, open the angular.json file and add the following line to it;

“node_modules/aos/dist/aos.css”

Having done that correctly, we have successfully installed and configured the AOS library, which makes it ready for use in our project.

We may need to restart our server in order for our project to be updated with recent changes after installation, but only if our project appears to be out of date.

3. Initializing/ Animating with the Animate On Scroll Library (AOS).

In this step, we would finally bring our animations to life and make them work as we scroll through our web pages. Let's get started and see what happens.
First, we must open our desired component's TS file, for example, “home-component.ts”, import the AOS library, and initialize it. This can be accomplished by following the steps outlined below;

  1. Import the library: Inside the desired component.ts file, add the import;
  1. Initialize the functionality: To get the AOS library functioning, it is important to call the init() function in the ngOnInit of our component.ts file. This can simply be done by adding the the following line of code:

By doing this, the AOS library has been initialized and our animations are ready for action. But before we can see the effects, we must open our component.html file(e.g home-component.html), which must be the html component of the ts file we just worked on, and set animation using the data-aos attribute in our desired divs. Example;


<div data-aos="fade-up" data-aos-duration="3000"> <!-- our contents —->
</div>

The code above would add a fade-up animation to the div on scroll, but the capability of the AOS Library is not limited to this. To discover more animations, The official Animate on Scroll website has an experience of animations and effects provided by the library. You may check it out here and notice how the effects happen as you scroll down and up the page.

Conclusion.

So far in this article, we've been able to see how easy it is to set up an Angular app with Animate On Scroll effects on its pages using the AOS Library. Questions and suggestions are always welcome in the comments. See you in the next article.
Happy Coding!

Thank you for reading this far. I hope you found the tutorial useful. If you have any questions or comments, please leave them in the comments section.

Items.Title: 每个开发人员都必须了解 CORS 的最低要求 | The minimum every developer must know about CORS
Items.Description:
越南

发表于它还指示服务器愿意支持的方法和标头（示例如下）。

  • 每当发出跨域请求时，大多数客户端浏览器都会强制执行 CORS。 如果请求调用外部源，则该请求是跨源的，这些源与服务于第一个资源的源不同。

    • 让我们看看下面的细节。

    简单的请求

    • 有关简单请求的完整定义，请参阅此处。 例如，没有标头的 GET 请求是一个简单的请求。

    • 一个简单的请求直接发送到服务器。 让我们在下面的示例中看看它是如何工作的：

      来源：MDN 网络文档

      • 请求 1：客户端浏览器首先从domain-a.com加载 Web 文档。 主请求将其origin定义为domain-a.com 。 然后在后续请求的请求标头中指定此来源。

         Origin: <origin>

      • 请求 2：客户端浏览器然后从domain-a.com向 GET 资源发送请求。 由于来自同一来源，所请求的资源由浏览器获取并呈现。

      • 请求 3：类似于请求 2，但请求现在被发送到domain-b.com ，这是一个不同的来源。

        • 资源将被成功获取，但是...
        • 仅当在响应标头中允许的来源 == domain-a.com （请求来源）或当它是通配符 (*) 时，它才可能由浏览器呈现，这意味着允许所有来源。
         Access-Control-Allow-Origin: <origin> | *

    任何不简单的请求都是“预先发送的”请求。

  • 与简单请求不同，浏览器会发送一个“预检”请求（OPTION 方法）来查看服务器是否允许实际的请求（“预检”请求）。


    • 来源：MDN 网络文档

    • 预检请求将包括以下标头：

    • 如果支持，则服务器响应具有以下标头：

    • 然后，浏览器会将响应头中的值与实际请求的相应信息进行比较。 如果全部允许，则发送实际请求； 否则，不是。

    • 预检请求也可以缓存在客户端，并在Access-Control-Max-Age响应标头中指示生存时间

    最后说明

    大多数浏览器都会这样做（以避免像 CSRF 这样的攻击）。

  • 一些开发工具没有（如 Postman）。

    • 进一步阅读和参考：

    本文仅介绍 CORS 的表面。 强烈推荐进一步阅读！

    在 Twitter 上关注我 ( @vietmle_ ) 以在我的新文章发布时获取更新！



    原文:

    Viet Le

    Posted on May 18 • Originally published at vietmle.com on May 18

    • CORS stands for Cross-Origin Resource Sharing.
    • CORS is HTTP-header based mechanism set by server to inform client side about allowed origins (<scheme>://<hostname>:<port>) other than its own. It also indicates method and headers which the server is willing to support (example included below).
    • Most of client browsers enforce CORS whenever a cross-origin request is made. A request is cross-origin if it calls to outside origins, which are different from the one served the first resource.

    CORS behaviour will be different between simple & “pre-flighted” requests. Let’s see the detail below.

    Simple requests

    • Refer here for full defition of simple request. For example, a GET request with no header is a simple request.

    • A simple request is sent directly to server. Let’s see how it works in the example below:

      Source: MDN web docs

      • Request 1: Client browser first load a web document from domain-a.com . The main request defines its origin as domain-a.com. This origin is then specified in the request header of subsequent requests.

        Origin: <origin>

      • Request 2: Client browser then send request to GET resource from domain-a.com. As comming from the same origin, the requested resource is fetched & rendered by browser.

      • Request 3: similar to request 2, but the request now is sent to domain-b.com , which is a different origin.

        • The resource will be successfully fetched, but ...
        • It might be rendered by the browser only if in the response header, the allowed origin == domain-a.com (the request origin) or when it is a wildcard (*) meaning permit all origin. 
        Access-Control-Allow-Origin: <origin> | *

    “Pre-flighted” requests

    • Any requests that are not simple is “pre-flighted” one.

    • Unlike simple request, the browser will send a “preflight” request (OPTION method) to see if the actual one ( ”pre-flighted” one ) is allowed by the server.


    Source: MDN web docs

    • A preflight request will include the following headers:

    • Server responses with the following headers if it supports:

    • Then, the browser will compare value in the response header against corresponding info of the actual request. If all is allowed, the actual request is sent; otherwise, not.

    • The preflight request can also be cached in client side with the time-to-live indicated in Access-Control-Max-Age response header

    Final note

    CORS is server-side security configurations that clients may enforce it.

    • Most browsers do (to avoid attack like CSRF).
    • Some dev tools do not (like Postman).

    Further reading & references:

    This article just covers surface of CORS. Further reading is highly recommended!

    Follow me (@vietmle_) on Twitter to get update whenever my new article is out!

Items.Title: Cómo escribir excelentes 提交 | Cómo escribir excelentes commits
Items.Description:
埃杰普洛：

feat(assets): se integran los assets para redes sociales. fix(Signin): se corrige inicio de sesión con facebook

类型

壮举： Una nueva funcionalidad。

  • 修复：Cuando se va a subir alguna corrección。
  • 文档：Solo para los cambios en los documentos。
  • 风格：Cuando el cambio no afecta el código o la funcionalidad（Espacios en blanco、formato、punto y coma 等），solo la estructura。
  • 重构： Cuando el cambio en el código no corrige un error o agrega una nueva funcionalidad。 范围
    学科
    Contiene la descripción del cambio, se debe de utilizar en tiempo presente, máximo 100 caracteres. Contiene la descripción del cambio，se debe de utilizar en tiempo presente，最大 100 字。

    Ultimos consejos: Ultimos consejos：

    • Escribe los comentarios en inglés, esto le dará un plus a tu -repositorio. Escribe los comentarios en inglés, esto le dará un plus a tu -repositorio。
    • Si trabajas con más personas, agrega esta información en el archivo de contribución para que tu equipo conozca como se tiene que crear los mensajes de los commits. Si trabajas con más personas, agrega esta informationación en el archivo de contribución para que tuequipo conozca como se tiene que crear los mensajes de los commits。
    • Tus commits deben de ser pequeños, es decir que contengan la menor cantidad de cambios, esto te ayudará a tener un mayor seguimiento de tus cambios. Tus commits deben de ser pequeños, es decir que contengan la menor cantidad de cambios, esto te ayudará a tener un Mayor seguimiento de tus cambios。

    Ya tienes todo para que tus proyectos sean muy profesionales, empieza ya a aplicar este formato a todos tus proyectos. Ya tienes todo para que tus proyectos sean muy profesionales, empieza ya a aplicar este formato a todos tus proyectos。 Compárteme en mis redes sociales como lo aplicaste en tus commits o si tienes alguna otra forma de hacerlo. Compárteme en mis redes sociales como lo aplicaste en tus commits o si tienes alguna otra forma de hacerlo。



    • 原文:

    Te ha pasado que cuando entras a un repositorio y buscas la lista de commits los comentarios son poco entendibles, no tienen una estructura definida o colocan mensajes como «se arregló error», pero estos comentarios no te aportan nada para entender el porqué del commit.

    Elaboré este pequeño tutorial para mostrarte un formato que puedes aplicar en los comentarios de tus commits, que te ayuden en la navegación y da una idea de primer vistazo de los cambios realizados dentro de los mismos.

    La estructura del mensaje es la siguiente:

    <type>(<scope>): <subject>

    Ejemplo:

    feat(assets): se integran los assets para redes sociales. fix(Signin): se corrige inicio de sesión con facebook

    Type

    En el tipo de commit tenemos las siguientes opciones para elegir:

    • feat: Una nueva funcionalidad.
    • fix: Cuando se va a subir alguna corrección.
    • docs: Solo para los cambios en los documentos.
    • style: Cuando el cambio no afecta el código o la funcionalidad (Espacios en blanco, formato, punto y coma, etc), solo la estructura.
    • refactor: Cuando el cambio en el código no corrige un error o agrega una nueva funcionalidad. Mejora en el código.

    Scope
    Es el ámbito donde se realizó el cambio, config, web-server, index, modulo, etc. Si estas trabajando en Gitlab con incidencias, puedes asociar fácilmente el comentario con la incidencia agregando # y el número de esta, te dejo un repo como referencia https://gitlab.com/kairos6/api-kairos.

    Subject
    Contiene la descripción del cambio, se debe de utilizar en tiempo presente, máximo 100 caracteres.

    Ultimos consejos:

    • Escribe los comentarios en inglés, esto le dará un plus a tu -repositorio.
    • Si trabajas con más personas, agrega esta información en el archivo de contribución para que tu equipo conozca como se tiene que crear los mensajes de los commits.
    • Tus commits deben de ser pequeños, es decir que contengan la menor cantidad de cambios, esto te ayudará a tener un mayor seguimiento de tus cambios.

    Ya tienes todo para que tus proyectos sean muy profesionales, empieza ya a aplicar este formato a todos tus proyectos. Compárteme en mis redes sociales como lo aplicaste en tus commits o si tienes alguna otra forma de hacerlo.

Items.Title: 开始使用 OSS | Getting Started With OSS
Items.Description:
巴布尼

发表于为开源做贡献是与其他开发人员协作和建立联系的绝佳方式。 它对冒名顶替综合症也有很大帮助； 它为您的第一份开发人员工作提供了很好的练习，并允许您逐步解决更具挑战性的任务。 此外，许多开源公司在招聘职位时会寻找他们的贡献者，因此您通过开源建立的联系可能对您的职业生涯很有价值。

我在做什么？

在问题中寻找低垂的果实，即容易改变和测试的东西。 如果没有大量问题，请尝试提高 repo 中的测试覆盖率。

我总是建议在提交拉取请求之前打开一个问题，以便您在实施这些更改之前获得有关您提议的更改的反馈。

如果我被卡住了怎么办

作为开发人员的一个非常重要的部分是学习导航代码库。 我喜欢使用ripgrep和Sourcegraph等工具在本地 repo (ripgrep) 或远程 repo (Sourcegraph) 中查找关键词。 两者都支持正则表达式，因此您可以非常具体地了解您正在寻找的序列。 我绝对建议您熟悉这些工具并学习回溯以找到解决方案。

当然，对于任何新的代码库，您都会遇到困难并需要帮助。 不用担心，大多数开源项目都有与之相关的社区，您可以在他们的 Github 或网站上轻松找到这些社区。 加入他们的社区并在 repo 上提出问题或作为问题或讨论提出问题是获得问题答案的好方法。 请注意，没有人会用勺子喂你，所以请确保你在适用时检查了你的语言的文档，并且你已经尝试通过搜索他们的代码和文档来回答你自己的问题。

如果对如何运行程序或将其设置为开发有任何不清楚的地方，请务必询问！

我需要知道什么？

不要忘记检查他们是否有一个contributing.md .md 文件，该文件指定问题、PR 等的格式。
您还可以查看以前合并的 PR，以了解过去接受的内容。

如果我错过了什么，请在评论中告诉我！

Davide 'CoderDave' Benvegnù - Davide 'CoderDave' Benvegnù - May 10 5月10日

Joao Carlos Sousa do Vale - Joao Carlos Sousa do Vale - Apr 21 4月21日



原文:

bashbunni

Posted on May 18

Contributing to open source is a fantastic way to collaborate and build connections with other developers. It also helps a lot with imposter syndrome; it gives you great practice for your first developer job and allows you to tackle progressively more challenging tasks. In addition, a lot of open source companies look to their contributors when hiring for a position, so the connections you build through open source can be valuable to your career.

What Do I Work On?

Look for low-hanging fruits in the issues, i.e. something easy enough to change and test. If there aren't tons of issues, then take a stab at improving test coverage in the repo.

I'd always recommend opening an issue before submitting a pull request so you get feedback on your proposed changes before going through the effort of implementing those changes.

What If I Get Stuck

A really important part of being a developer is learning to navigate a code base. I enjoy using tools like ripgrep and Sourcegraph for finding key words in either a local repo (ripgrep) or remote repo (Sourcegraph). Both support regular expressions, so you can get very specific with the sequences you're looking for. I definitely recommend getting comfortable with these tools and learning to backtrack to find solutions.

Of course, with any new code base you'll get stuck and need help. Don't worry, most open source projects have communities associated with them that you can find easily on their Github or website. Joining their community and asking questions or asking questions as issues or discussions on the repo are great ways to get answers to your questions. Note that nobody is going to spoon-feed you, so make sure you've checked the docs for your language when applicable and that you've tried to answer your own question by searching through their code and documentation.

If anything is unclear about how to run the program or get it set up for development, definitely ask!

What Do I Need to Know?

Don't forget to check if they have a contributing.md file that specifies the format for issues, PRs, etc.
You can also look at previous PRs that have been merged to get an idea of what has been accepted in the past.

*nice to know, not need to know

Let me know in the comments if there's anything I missed!

Davide 'CoderDave' Benvegnù - May 10

Joao Carlos Sousa do Vale - Apr 21

Items.Title: Golang básico - Declarando variáveis | Golang básico - Declarando variáveis
Items.Description:
变量声明

例：

 package main import ( "fmt" "runtime" ) func main () { var valor int if runtime . NumCPU () > 0 { valor = runtime . NumCPU () } fmt . Println ( valor ) }

变量组

没有 nosso caso valor e categoria 。
简历

  • 简化和表达！


原文:

Esse post faz parte de uma série onde pretendo compartilhar o básico essencial para desenvolver com GoLang.

A maneira mais simples de entender variáveis é lembrar que um programa funciona com entrada -> processamento -> saída. Declarar variáveis é a forma que temos de reservar espaços em memória para armazenar as entradas de dados e permitir que processamento use essas variáveis e produza algum resultado como saída.

Declarando variáveis

Em Go existem algumas maneiras de fazer isso, todas elas tem um contexto e saber onde usar vai simplificar bastante seu código.

Utilizando var com tipo explicito

Esse é tipo de declaração que usamos no inicio de uma função, quando você declara esse tipo de variável você está deixando explicito o tipo da varíavel que nos blocos seguintes vai ter o seu valor modificado (ou não). Por exemplo:

package main import ( "fmt" "runtime"
) func main() { var valor int if runtime.NumCPU() > 0 { valor = runtime.NumCPU() } fmt.Println(valor)
}

Nesse código estou declarando a variável valor de tipo int(ou seja, um número inteiro). E logo em seguida eu faço um processamento para atribuir um novo valor a essa variável.

Dica: você pode testar esse código no https://go.dev/play/

Criando e atribuindo de forma implícita

Criar variáveis implícitas é provavelmente o jeito mais fácil e prático, mas sempre se atente ao contexto. É muito comum elas serem usados dentro dos blocos, e elas normalmente são resultado da chamada a uma função ou do resultado de uma expressão, vamos ver nosso exemplo novamente:

package main import ( "fmt" "runtime"
) func main() { var valor int if runtime.NumCPU() > 0 { valor = runtime.NumCPU() } maiorQueDois := runtime.NumCPU() > 2 fmt.Println(valor, maiorQueDois)
}

Agora nós estamos criando a variável maiorQueDois, ela é resultado da expressão runtime.NumCPU() > 2 e implicitamente o Go decide que o tipo dessa variável é bool.

Grupos de variáveis

O última forma que vamos abordar é o grupo de variáveis:

package main import ( "fmt" "runtime"
) func main() { var ( valor = 4 categoria = "brabo" ) if runtime.NumCPU() > 0 { valor = runtime.NumCPU() } maiorQueDois := valor > 2 if valor <= 4 { categoria = "mais ou menos" } fmt.Println(valor, maiorQueDois, categoria)
}

Grupos são uma combinação das duas formas, precisamos do var, seguido de parêntesis e as variáveis separadas por linhas. No nosso caso valor e categoria.
Perceba, quando você cria um grupo você está expressando que essas variáveis tem alguma conexão, no meu exemplo a categoria é dependente do valor.

Resumo

  • Declaração explicita é feita com var nomeVariavel tipo
  • Declaração implícita utiliza nomeVariavel := expressão
  • Grupo de variáveis utilizam: 
var (
   nomeVariavel = `valor` ou `expressão`
)
  • Go é uma linguagem muito simples e de poucos comandos, sempre que for usar um tipo de declaração tente expressar sua intenção. Simplicidade e expressividade!
