Items.Title: 修复 NextJS<Image> 覆盖其他组件 | Fixing NextJS <Image> covering other components
Items.Description:
嘿伙计们，第一次在这里发布海报。 我一直在为我的组织开发一个项目，并且一直在使用它来帮助学习 NextJS 和 CSS。

这个项目是我第一次使用 NextJS 的真实体验，我真的很喜欢它，尤其是来自 React。 话虽如此，整个体验并不完美。 在这里，我将谈谈我最近遇到的一个问题以及我是如何解决它的，希望如果你发现自己处于同样的情况，你可能能够克服它。

如果您不确定该组件是什么或做什么，请参阅以下文档：
简而言之，当您调用该组件时，它将有助于优化您链接到的图像，无论是公用文件夹中的静态图像还是 URL。 我喜欢它的地方是我不必搞乱图像的分辨率。 我可以简单地指定布局、高度、宽度、质量，然后就可以了。 这是我正在处理的卡代码中的一个示例。 此外，意大利面条代码警告。

 import { useState } from 'react' import style from './Card.module.css' import Image from 'next/image' import Link from 'next/link' const Card = ({ name, path, img, requireCac, description }) => { const [showDescription, setShowDescription] = useState(false) const toggleDescription = () => { setShowDescription(true) } const hideDescription = () => { setShowDescription(false) } return ( <> <Link href={path} passHref> <div className={style.cardContainer} onMouseLeave={hideDescription}> <section className={style.imageBlock}> <div className={style.imageContainer} onMouseEnter={toggleDescription}> <Image src={img} alt='The Law' layout='responsive' width={5000} height={2815} quality={100} sizes={'100vw'} className={style.cardImage} /> </div> {showDescription ? ( <p className={style.cardDescription} onMouseEnter={toggleDescription}> {description} </p> ) : null} </section> <section className={style.cardInfoBlock}> <div> <h3 className={style.cardName}>{name}</h3> </div> {requireCac ? ( <div> <p className={style.requiresCacBox}>CAC Required</p> </div> ) : null} </section> </div> </Link> </> ) } export default Card

问题

对我来说，它做得很好，卡片渲染得很好。

不幸的是，当我进入移动视图时出现了一个小问题

对我来说，这不是我第一次遇到这个问题。 我之前一直在主页上工作，并且一直遇到覆盖所有内容的问题，并且图像覆盖了所有内容。 在深入挖掘组件的开发工具时，我发现它是一组围绕绝对定位的跨度酷甜，它只需要一个亲戚来锁定：

不

所以那没有用。 然后我继续尝试更多的“位置：相对”飞镖投掷，这自然不起作用，我最终打破了我的移动导航菜单，这需要一些时间来实现和修复。

我做了一些谷歌搜索，我发现了一个关于覆盖内容的背景图像的 Stackoverflow（我现在无法找到），我在那里尝试了修复，令人惊讶的是，这是另一个“位置：相对”尝试，它也没有不工作。

我找到了另一个可能的答案，我找不到链接，建议您将父容器设置为 position: absolute 和 Z-Index: -1，这解决了问题但产生了一个新问题，卡片有效在主要的 html 正文下。 由于页脚覆盖了底部的卡片，这基本上在滚动到最终项目时产生了问题，并且尝试选择元素被证明是一种痛苦，因为它位于父组件和元素的下方（Z 方向）选择器工具一直选择父卡而不是单个卡。

所以，这并没有真正起作用，但它确实让我想到了另一个想法，在修复了谷歌搜索之后，我发现了这个：
底部的答案已经尝试过（设置父母

到位置：相对，所以被忽略了。 我最终做的是查看 AnanyaGBs 的答案，然后我开始搜索移动导航代码以寻找可行的方法。

长话短说，我的导航栏中有一个 .mobileItems 类名。 我最初将其设置为粘性，以便在用户浏览网站时跟随用户。 我最终改变了主意，并在导航菜单打开时锁定滚动，但我从未删除位置属性。 我试图将其更改为绝对，但由于某种原因破坏了导航栏的呈现，它只会显示一个导航项而不是所有导航项。 我最终将其保留为粘性，并将 Z-Index 设置为 99，瞧。

结论

因此，下次当您遇到 NextJS 组件位于某物之上的问题时，与其尝试将其包装在 a 中并为其添加“位置：相对”，请查看是否可以将非静态位置属性设置为它所在的项目并给它一个高 Z 指数。 它必须是非静态的（绝对的、粘性的或固定的），否则 Z-Index 将不起作用。

感谢您花时间阅读本文，希望对您有所帮助。



原文:

Hey folks, first time poster here. I've been working on a project for my organization and I've been using it to help learn NextJS and CSS.

This project has been my first real experience with NextJS and I've been really enjoying it, especially coming from React. That being said, the whole experience hasn't been perfect. Here I'll talk about a recent issue I ran into and how I was able to solve it so hopefully if you find yourself in the same situation, you may be able to get past it.

NextJS Image

If you are unsure of what the component is or does here's the documentation:
https://nextjs.org/docs/api-reference/next/image#loader

In a nutshell, when you call the component it will help optimize the image you link it to, whether its a static image in the public folder, or a URL. What I like about it is that I don't have to mess around with the resolution of the image. I can just simply specify the layout, height, width, the quality, and it'll go from there. Here's an example from my code of the card I'm working on. Also, spaghetti code warning.

import { useState } from 'react'
import style from './Card.module.css'
import Image from 'next/image'
import Link from 'next/link'

const Card = ({ name, path, img, requireCac, description }) => {
    const [showDescription, setShowDescription] = useState(false)

    const toggleDescription = () => {
        setShowDescription(true)
    }

    const hideDescription = () => {
        setShowDescription(false)
    }

    return (
        <>
            <Link href={path} passHref>
                <div
                    className={style.cardContainer}
                    onMouseLeave={hideDescription}>
                    <section className={style.imageBlock}>
                        <div
                            className={style.imageContainer}
                            onMouseEnter={toggleDescription}>
                            <Image
                                src={img}
                                alt='The Law'
                                layout='responsive'
                                width={5000}
                                height={2815}
                                quality={100}
                                sizes={'100vw'}
                                className={style.cardImage}
                            />
                        </div>

                        {showDescription ? (
                            <p
                                className={style.cardDescription}
                                onMouseEnter={toggleDescription}>
                                {description}
                            </p>
                        ) : null}
                    </section>

                    <section className={style.cardInfoBlock}>
                        <div>
                            <h3 className={style.cardName}>{name}</h3>
                        </div>

                        {requireCac ? (
                            <div>
                                <p className={style.requiresCacBox}>CAC Required</p>
                            </div>
                        ) : null}
                    </section>
                </div>
            </Link>
        </>
    )
}

export default Card

Problem

For me, it did the trick well and the cards are rendering nicely.

Unfortunately, there is a small problem when I go to the mobile view

For me this wasn't the first time I ran into this issue. I had previously been working on the home page and kept having problems with the overriding everything, and it was to a point where the images were covering everything. While digging deep into the Dev Tools for the component I discovered it was a set of spans enveloped around an absolutely positioned (image) tag.

Cool sweet, it just needs a relative to latch on to:

NOPE

So that didn't work. I then proceeded to try a few more 'position: relative' dart throws, which naturally didn't work and I ended up breaking my mobile navigation menu which took some time to realize and to fix.

I did some googling, I found a Stackoverflow (which I'm not currently able to find now ) about a background image covering content and I tried the fix there, which surprise, is another 'position:relative' attempt, which also didn't work.

I found another potential answer, which I can't find a link for, that recommended you set the parent container to position: absolute and the Z-Index: -1, which solved the problem but created a new problem, the cards were effectively under the main html body. This basically created a problem when it came to scrolling to the final item thanks to the footer covering the card at the bottom, and attempting to select the element proved to be a pain because it was underneath (Z wise) the parent component and the element selector tool kept selecting the parent rather than the individual cards.

So, that didn't really work, but it did lead me to another idea and after fixing up the googling, I found this:
https://github.com/vercel/next.js/discussions/30259

The answer at the bottom had already been attempted (setting a parent

to position:relative, so that was ignored. What I ended up doing was looking at the AnanyaGBs answer, and I began scouring the mobile Nav code for something that would work.

Long story short, I had a .mobileItems className in my nav bar. I had originally set it to sticky so it would follow the user as he/she navigated down the site. I eventually changed my mind on this and made it where the scrolling is locked when the navigation menu is open, but I never removed the position property. I tried to change it to absolute, but that for some reason destroyed the rendering of the navigation bar and it would only show one of the navigation items instead of all of them. I ultimately left it on sticky, and set the Z-Index to 99, and voila.

Conclusion

So next time you are running into a problem with the NextJS component sitting on top of something, instead of trying to wrap it in a and adding 'position:relative' to it, see if you can set a non-static position property to the item it's sitting on and give it a high Z-Index. It has to be non-static(absolute, sticky, or fixed), otherwise Z-Index will have no effect.

Thanks for taking the time to read this, hopefully it was helpful.
Items.Author: abernachy

Items.Title: 拼写检查 (GitHub) 操作的 0.24.0 版 - 一个引入可选工件的功能版本 | Release 0.24.0 of Spellcheck (GitHub) Action - a feature release introducing optional artifacts
Items.Description:
我很高兴地宣布Spellcheck GitHub Action - 0.24.0的新功能发布。

该功能版本引入了生成工件的能力，该工件具有运行运行对动作的核心组件进行pyspelling的所有输出。

这个想法是由 Riccardo Porreca ( @riccardoporreca ) 提出的，他为0.18.0版本做出了贡献。

我开始调查这个想法，根本没有使用 GitHub 动作工件的经验。 我让它工作了，但必须推动我的初始更改才能看到它工作，这意味着主分支变得不稳定（我们在文档中声明），但我没有完全成功，因为我可以指定和生成工件，但我无法从 Docker 映像中提取它。 我可以在本地使用 Docker 时检索工件，但这是 GitHub 操作，因此需要更多操作。

我以身份联系了 Riccardo，幸运的是，他对该主题非常了解，我们能够调整解决方案。

此外，Riccardo 还为我提供了很多改进建议，我开始实施它们，在进行过程中打破了一切。 我继续测试和改进代码并让它工作。 只是看到里卡多指出了我犯的所有错误。 好吧，我们最终达到了同一点，但如果我注意我的通知而不是分区（感谢“无人机区”），本可以节省一些时间。

感谢 Riccardo 的最初想法、评论、评论和建议。

关于工件的想法，好吧，我不知道 Riccardo 想要做什么，但我可以看到一个用例，您可以在其中检索输出并处理它以使用您首选的工具链修复拼写错误。

该工件当然可以通过 GitHub UI 下载。 但是，您也可以通过 API 访问它。

以下示例使用GitHub CLI 客户端（文档），但可以使用curl或类似的，请参阅参考文档了解详细信息。

首先，您配置您想要一个工件：

 name : Spellcheck Action on : push jobs : build : name : Spellcheck runs-on : ubuntu-latest steps : # The checkout step - uses : actions/checkout@master - uses : rojopolis/spellcheck-github-actions@0.24.0 name : Spellcheck with : source_files : README.md CHANGELOG.md notes/Notes.md task_name : Markdown output_file : spellcheck-output.txt

一个有趣的新参数是output_file ，它既可以作为启动器，也可以指定工件的名称。

 output_file : spellcheck-output.txt

其次，你运行一个测试。

 gh workflow run "Spellcheck Action"

注意！ 您的拼写操作名称可能会有所不同。

然后你询问是否有任何文物。

 gh api \ -H "Accept: application/vnd.github.v3+json" \ /repos/rojopolis/spellcheck-github-actions/actions/artifacts

从 JSON 响应中，您提取所需工件的 ID。

然后下载工件。

 gh api \ -H "Accept: application/vnd.github.v3+json" \ /repos/rojopolis/spellcheck-github-actions/actions/artifacts/243391075,/zip > artifact.zip

拆开工件。

检查工件（在配置中指定的spellcheck-output.txt ）。

 cat spellcheck-output.txt Spelling check passed : )

如果您的自定义词典有多个拼写错误或候选者，结果当然会更有趣 - 如果您想要大量输出或暂时清空.wordlist.txt ，请切换语言。

工件可使用 3 个月，并且可以通过 API（以及我相信的 UI）列出、查看和删除。

当然，欢迎在此处以及GitHub 存储库的“讨论”部分中提供有关此新功能的反馈和问题。

更改日志

在 0.24.0 版本中，现在可用。

动作配置必须指定output_file参数，这是一个新的可选参数。

 name : Spellcheck Action on : workflow_dispatch : push : jobs : build : name : Spellcheck runs-on : ubuntu-latest steps : - uses : actions/checkout@v3 - uses : rojopolis/spellcheck-github-actions@0.24.0 name : Spellcheck (no output file) with : source_files : README.md CHANGELOG.md - uses : rojopolis/spellcheck-github-actions@0.24.0 name : Spellcheck (with output file) with : source_files : README.md CHANGELOG.md task_name : Markdown output_file : spellcheck-output.txt - uses : actions/upload-artifact@v3 name : Archive spellcheck output output with : name : Spellcheck artifact path : spellcheck-output.txt

这介绍了upload-artifact@v3操作的使用。

生成的工件可以通过 GitHub UI/API 下载，详细信息和指针请查阅文档。

感谢@riccardoporreca 的建议以及@jonasbn对 PR 的额外评论和评论

该版本将提供 365 天，新的日落政策



原文:

I am happy to announce a new feature release of Spellcheck GitHub Action - 0.24.0.

The feature release introduces the ability to generate an artifact with all the output from the run of the action of pyspelling the core component of the action.

The idea was proposed by Riccardo Porreca (@riccardoporreca), who has contributed to version 0.18.0.

I started investigating the idea, having no experience with working with GitHub action artifacts at all. I got it working, but had to push my initial changes to see it work, which meant that the master branch went unstable (something we state in the documentation), I did however not succeed entirely, since I could specify and generate the artifact, but I could not extract it from the Docker image. I could retrieve the artifact when using Docker locally, but this is a GitHub action, so a little more was required.

I reached out to Riccardo with a status and luckily he is quite knowledgeable on the topioc and we where able to adjust the solution.

In addition Riccardo had provided me with a quite a few pointers for improvements and I started to implement them, breaking everything as I went along. I continued to test and improve the code and got it working. Only to see that Riccardo had pointed out all the errors I had made. Well we got to the same point eventually, but could have save sometime had I paid attention to my notification instead of zoning out (thanks "Drone Zone").

Thanks to Riccardo for the initial idea, reviews, comments and suggestions.

The idea with the artifact, well I do not know what Riccardo want to do, but I can see a use case where you can retrieve the output and process it to fix your spelling errors using your preferred toolchain.

The artifact can of course be downloaded via the GitHub UI. You can however also access it via the API.

The following examples are using the GitHub CLI client (documentation), but curl or similar can be used, consult the referenced documentation for details.

First you configure that you want an artifact:

name: Spellcheck Action
on: push jobs: build: name: Spellcheck runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: # The checkout step - uses: actions/checkout@master - uses: rojopolis/spellcheck-github-actions@0.24.0 name: Spellcheck with: source_files: README.md CHANGELOG.md notes/Notes.md task_name: Markdown output_file: spellcheck-output.txt

The new an interesting parameter is output_file this acts both as an enabler and you can also specify the name of your artifact.

output_file: spellcheck-output.txt

Second, you run a test.

gh workflow run "Spellcheck Action"

NB! your name of the spellchech action might vary.

Then you inquire if there are any artifacts.

gh api \ -H "Accept: application/vnd.github.v3+json" \
  /repos/rojopolis/spellcheck-github-actions/actions/artifacts

From the JSON response, you extract the ID of the artifact you want.

Then you download the artifact.

gh api \ -H "Accept: application/vnd.github.v3+json" \ /repos/rojopolis/spellcheck-github-actions/actions/artifacts/243391075,/zip > artifact.zip

Unpack the artifact.

Inspect the artifact (spellcheck-output.txt as specified in the configuration).

cat spellcheck-output.txt
Spelling check passed :)

The result is of course more interesting if you have several spelling mistakes or candidates for your custom dictionary - switch language if your want a lot of output or temporarily empty your .wordlist.txt.

Artifacts are available for 3 months and can be listed, viewed and deleted via the APIs (and the UI I believe).

Feedback and questions about this new feature is of course welcome, here as well as in the Discussions section in the GitHub repository.

Change Log

0.24.0, 2022-05-17, feature release, update not required

  • @riccardoporreca created issue #68 requested the ability to create an output artifact.

With release 0.24.0 this is now available.

The action configuration has to have the output_file parameter specified, which is a new optional parameter.

 name: Spellcheck Action on: workflow_dispatch: push: jobs: build: name: Spellcheck runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v3 - uses: rojopolis/spellcheck-github-actions@0.24.0 name: Spellcheck (no output file) with: source_files: README.md CHANGELOG.md - uses: rojopolis/spellcheck-github-actions@0.24.0 name: Spellcheck (with output file) with: source_files: README.md CHANGELOG.md task_name: Markdown output_file: spellcheck-output.txt - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v3 name: Archive spellcheck output output with: name: Spellcheck artifact path: spellcheck-output.txt

This introduces the use of the upload-artifact@v3 action.

The generated artifact can be downloaded via GitHub UI/API, please consult the documentation for details and pointers.

Thanks to @riccardoporreca for his suggestion and additional review and comments on PR by @jonasbn

The release will be available for 365 days, with the new sunset policy

Items.Title: 六大 Kubernetes 车队管理最佳实践 | Top Six Kubernetes Best Practices for Fleet Management
Items.Description:
配置 Kubernetes 集群相对容易。 但是，每个新集群都是漫长旅程的开始，而您添加到Kubernetes 队列的每个集群都会增加管理复杂性。 此外，许多企业难以跟上分布在本地、云和边缘位置的 Kubernetes 集群数量的快速增长——通常具有不同的 Kubernetes 配置并在不同环境中使用不同的工具。

幸运的是，有许多 K8s 最佳实践可以帮助您控制混乱，提高 Kubernetes 的成功率，并为应对快速增长和动态的 Kubernetes 需求做好准备。 本博客描述了六种战略性 Kubernetes 最佳实践，它们将使您走上成功管理车队的道路。

最佳实践 1：考虑混合云和多云

这场流行病已经成为各种规模的组织的现实检验，确立了云计算和云原生开发的价值并加速了它们的采用。

由于 Kubernetes 工作负载具有高度可移植性，您可以选择在任何云中部署工作负载，以为您的客户和员工提供最佳体验——其中最佳可能意味着具有最低网络延迟的最佳性能或利用差异化服务原生的能力到特定的云。

虽然混合和多云 Kubernetes 集群部署为您的业务提供了优势并已成为最佳实践，但它们增加了 Kubernetes 集群的复杂性。 但是，正确的 SaaS 工具在混合和多云环境中具有显着优势，使您能够以更少的摩擦跨多个公共云和数据中心环境进行操作，同时实现更高水平的标准化。

最佳实践 2：强调自动化

当您只有几个集群时，使用 kubectl 命令和一些脚本管理 Kubernetes 并不太难，但它根本无法扩展。 自动化和标准化常见的集群和应用程序操作使您可以更轻松地管理更多集群，同时避免由于人为错误而导致的错误配置。 出于这个原因，自动化被认为是控制 Kubernetes 队列的最佳实践。

许多组织正在采用 GitOps，将 Git 工具的熟悉功能引入基础架构管理和持续部署 (CD)。 在去年的AWS 容器安全调查中，64.5% 的受访者表示他们已经在使用 GitOps。

使用GitOps ，当对 Git 存储库进行更改时，代码会被推送到（或从）生产基础设施中回滚，从而快速可靠地自动化部署。 知道。

但 GitOps 并不是增加 Kubernetes 自动化的唯一工具。 一些 Kubernetes 管理工具利用开放策略代理(OPA)，这是一种通用策略引擎，用于在微服务、Kubernetes、CI/CD 管道、API 网关等中实施策略。OPA 可用于在整个系统中启用基于策略的管理K8s 车队。 请参阅使用 OPA Gatekeeper 在 Kubernetes 上管理策略以了解更多信息。

最佳实践 3：安全性“零”

您的 Kubernetes 机群的安全性永远不应成为事后的想法。 在生产中运行的关键任务集群和应用程序需要最高级别的安全性和控制。 此外，随着您的车队增长，您的企业可能会面临新的安全风险。

不幸的是，保持所有单个元素在数十个集群中正确配置和对齐是一个巨大的挑战。

最佳实践 4：最大化监控

然而，就像其他一切一样，随着集群数量和不同云环境的增加，监控和可见性变得更具挑战性。

最佳实践是在整个 Kubernetes 队列中提供具有基本监控、警报和可视化级别的集中式日志记录。 许多组织使用 Prometheus 和 Grafana 等开源工具自行实现这一点。

但是，为了与之前的最佳实践保持一致，SaaS 服务将为您完成繁重的工作，在一个地方提供您需要的一切，并在不同的环境中提供统一的工具。 您可以在最近的博客“Kubernetes 监控的最佳实践、工具和方法”中了解有关可见性和监控的更多信息。

最佳实践 5：选择软件即服务

一个完整的 Kubernetes 环境有很多活动部分。 一旦您添加了开发人员工具、管理工具、监控、安全服务等，这是对时间和精力的重大且持续的投资； 可能需要大量技能才能使所有工具保持最新并按预期运行。 因此，随着您的环境扩展，最好让服务代替您必须尽可能安装和管理自己的软件。

托管 Kubernetes
例如，大多数企业利用 AWS (EKS)、Azure (AKS) 和谷歌云 (GKE) 等公共云提供商提供的托管 Kubernetes 服务来简化集群部署，将应用程序定位在更靠近客户的位置，并提供动态部署的能力。在不需要大量资本支出的情况下扩大规模以解决峰值负载。 大多数公共云还提供各种易于使用的相关服务，补充您的 Kubernetes 工作并加速开发。

此外，越来越多的软件即服务 (SaaS) 和托管解决方案提供 Kubernetes 管理、监控、安全和其他功能。 特别是 SaaS 模型提供了快速的价值实现时间、稳健性和可靠性、灵活的定价和易用性。

选择 SaaS 工具来满足业务和运营需求可以让您减少对难以找到的技术专家的依赖。 还值得注意的是，尽管我们中的许多人在某种程度上可以互换使用“托管”和“SaaS”这两个术语， 但它们并不是一回事。 做出明智的选择。

最佳实践 6：DI-为什么？

当您的组织刚刚开始使用 Kubernetes 时，自己动手 (DIY) 可能是有意义的——为 Kubernetes 构建专用基础架构、编译上游代码和开发内部工具——但随着 Kubernetes 对您的生产越来越重要操作，它不能再被视为一个科学项目。 那么为什么要让 Kubernetes 变得更难呢？

围绕 Kubernetes 出现了一个完整的服务、支持和工具生态系统，以帮助简化从部署到开发再到运营的一切。 正确的服务、工具和合作伙伴将使您以更少的劳力完成更多工作。 继续推出 Kubernetes 是在浪费开发人员和运营人员的时间和人才，而这些时间和人才本可以用于在业务的其他地方增加更多价值。

在 Rafay，我们围绕编码 Kubernetes 最佳实践构建了整个平台，以简化对大型 K8s 队列的管理。 我们的Kubernetes 运营平台(KOP) 统一了集群和容器化应用程序的生命周期管理，并结合了讨论的所有最佳实践：

软件即服务： Rafay 的 SaaS 优先方法使您的组织几乎可以立即从 Kubernetes 中获得效率，加快数字化转型计划，同时保持较低的运营成本。
混合云和多云： Rafay KOP 可跨云、数据中心和边缘环境工作，让您可以在任何需要的地方轻松部署和操作工作负载。
监控和可见性： Rafay 的可见性和监控服务使可视化、监控和管理集群和应用程序的运行状况变得简单。
自动化： Rafay 使用我们的GitOps 服务简化自动化。 我们的多集群管理服务包含集群模板、集群蓝图和工作负载模板——确保遵守 Kubernetes 部署最佳实践——以及我们利用 OPA 的 Kubernetes 策略管理服务。
安全性： Rafay 的零信任访问服务通过自动化 RBAC 对整个车队进行集中访问控制。 它确保在多集群、多云部署中应用和维护 Kubernetes 安全最佳实践。

准备好了解为什么这么多企业和平台团队与 Rafay 合作进行 Kubernetes 车队管理了吗？ 注册免费试用。



原文:

Provisioning a Kubernetes cluster is relatively easy. However, each new cluster is the beginning of a very long journey, and every cluster you add to your Kubernetes fleet increases management complexity. In addition, many enterprises struggle to keep up with a rapidly growing number of Kubernetes clusters spread across on-prem, cloud, and edge locations — often with diverse Kubernetes configs and using different tools in different environments.

Fortunately, there are a number of K8s best practices that will help you rein in the chaos, increase your Kubernetes success, and prepare you to cope with fast-growing and dynamic Kubernetes requirements. This blog describes six strategic Kubernetes best practices that will put you on the path to successfully managing a fleet.

Best Practice 1: Think Hybrid and Multi-Cloud

The pandemic has been a reality check for organizations of all sizes, establishing the value of cloud computing and cloud-native development and accelerating their adoption.

Because Kubernetes workloads can be highly portable, you have the option to deploy workloads in any cloud to deliver an optimal experience for your customers and employees — where optimal may mean the best performance with the lowest network latency or the ability to leverage a differentiated service native to a particular cloud.

While hybrid and multi-cloud Kubernetes cluster deployments provide advantages to your business and have become a best practice, they increase the complexity of your Kubernetes fleet. However, the right SaaS tools offer significant advantages in hybrid and multi-cloud environments, enabling you to operate across multiple public clouds and data center environments with less friction while allowing a greater level of standardization.

Best Practice 2: Emphasize Automation

Managing Kubernetes with kubectl commands and a few scripts is not too difficult when you only have a few clusters, but it simply doesn’t scale. Automating and standardizing common cluster and application operations allows you to manage more clusters with less effort while avoiding misconfigurations due to human errors. For this reason, automation is considered a best practice for gaining control of your Kubernetes fleet.

Many organizations are adopting GitOps, bringing the familiar capabilities of Git tools to infrastructure management and continuous deployment (CD). In last year’s AWS Container Security Survey, 64.5% of the respondents indicated they were using GitOps already.

With GitOps, when changes are made to a Git repository, code is pushed to (or rolled back from) the production infrastructure, thus automating deployments quickly and reliably.GitOps was the subject of the recent Rafay Blog, GitOps Principles and Workflows Every Team Should Know.

But GitOps is not the only tool to increase Kubernetes automation. Some Kubernetes management tools utilize Open Policy Agent (OPA), a general-purpose policy engine used to enforce policies in microservices, Kubernetes, CI/CD pipelines, API gateways, etc. OPA can be used to enable policy-based management across your entire K8s fleet. See Managing Policies on Kubernetes using OPA Gatekeeper to learn more.

Best Practice 3: “Zero” In on Security

Security for your Kubernetes fleet should never be an afterthought. Mission-critical clusters and applications running in production require the highest level of security and control. In addition, as your fleet grows, your enterprise may be exposed to new security risks.

Applying zero-trust principles is the best practice for securing your K8s environment. Kubernetes includes all the hooks necessary for zero-trust. Unfortunately, keeping all the individual elements correctly configured and aligned across dozens of clusters is a big challenge.

Best Practice 4: Maximize Monitoring

There are a variety of open source solutions for Kubernetes monitoring. However, just like with everything else, monitoring and visibility become more challenging as the number of clusters and different cloud environments increase.

The best practice is to provide centralized logging with a base level of monitoring, alerting, and visualization across your Kubernetes fleet. Many organizations implement this on their own using open source tools such as Prometheus and Grafana.

However, in keeping with the previous best practice, there are SaaS services that will do the heavy lifting for you, providing everything you need in one place with uniform tools across diverse environments. You can learn more about visibility and monitoring in the recent blog, Best Practices, Tools, and Approaches for Kubernetes Monitoring.

Best Practice 5: Opt for Software as a Service

A complete Kubernetes environment has a lot of moving parts. Once you add developer tools, management tools, monitoring, security services, etc., it’s a significant and ongoing investment in time and energy; Substantial skill may be needed to keep all the tools up-to-date and operating as expected. Therefore, as your environment scales, it’s best to let services take the place of software you have to install and manage yourself wherever possible.

Managed Kubernetes
For example, most enterprises make use of managed Kubernetes services from public cloud providers such as AWS (EKS), Azure (AKS), and Google Cloud (GKE) to simplify cluster deployment, position applications closer to customers, and provide the ability to dynamically scale up to address peak loads without requiring a lot of CapEx. A recently released study from the CNCF found that 79% of respondents use public cloud Kubernetes services. Most public clouds also offer various related services that are easy to consume, complement your Kubernetes efforts, and accelerate development.

SaaS tools
In addition, there are an increasing number of software-as-a-service (SaaS) and hosted solutions that provide Kubernetes management, monitoring, security, and other capabilities. The SaaS model, in particular, provides fast time to value, robustness and reliability, flexible pricing, and ease of use.

Choosing SaaS tools to address business and operational needs can enable you to reduce reliance on hard-to-find technical experts. It’s also worth noting that, although many of us use the terms “hosted” and “SaaS” somewhat interchangeably, they are not the same thing. Choose wisely.

Best Practice 6: DI-Why?

Kubernetes has a reputation for being challenging to deploy and operate. When your organization was just getting started with Kubernetes, it may have made sense to do it yourself (DIY) — building dedicated infrastructure for Kubernetes, compiling upstream code, and developing your internal tools — but as Kubernetes becomes more and more critical to your production operations, it can no longer be treated as a science project. So why make Kubernetes harder than it has to be?

An entire ecosystem of services, support, and tools is arising around Kubernetes to help simplify everything from deployment to development to operations. The right services, tools, and partners will allow you to accomplish more — with much less toil. Continuing to roll your Kubernetes is a waste of developer and operations time and talent that could be spent adding more value elsewhere in your business.

Kubernetes Best Practices at Rafay

At Rafay we’ve built our entire platform around codifying Kubernetes best practices in order to streamline management of large K8s fleets. Our Kubernetes Operations Platform (KOP) unifies lifecycle management for both clusters and containerized applications, incorporating all of the best practices discussed:

Software as a Service: Rafay’s SaaS-first approach enables your organization to gain efficiencies from Kubernetes almost immediately, speeding digital transformation initiatives while keeping operating costs low.
Hybrid and multi-cloud: Rafay KOP works across cloud, data center, and edge environments, allowing you to easily deploy and operate workloads wherever needed.
Monitoring and Visibility: Rafay’s Visibility and Monitoring Service makes it simple to visualize, monitor, and manage the health of your clusters and applications.
Automation: Rafay simplifies automation with our GitOps Service. Our Multi-Cluster Management Service incorporates cluster templates, cluster blueprints, and workload templates — ensuring adherence to Kubernetes deployment best practices — as well as our Kubernetes Policy Management Service utilizing OPA.
Security: Rafay’s Zero-Trust Access Service centralizes access control for your entire fleet with automated RBAC. It ensures that Kubernetes security best practices are applied and maintained in multi-cluster, multi-cloud deployments.

Rafay delivers the fleet management capabilities you need to ensure the success of your Kubernetes environment, helping you rationalize and standardize management across your entire fleet of K8s clusters and applications.

Ready to find out why so many enterprises and platform teams have partnered with Rafay for Kubernetes fleet management? Sign up for a free trial.

Items.Title: 云简历挑战 | Cloud Resume Challenge
Items.Description:
我在运营技术（发电厂和炼油厂的工业控制系统）领域工作了大约 15 年，从最初的建设和调试，到维护和现代化设计审查，无所不包。 最近决定转行信息安全，通过学习和实施项目，我爱上了云服务。 这一发现促使我开始研究 AWS 服务，这让我找到了Cloud Resume Challenge 。

背景

该项目是由从事 DevOps 和云工程的 Forrest Brazeal 创建的挑战。 面临的挑战是使用大量云服务构建一个简单、静态的简历网站。 由于挑战允许您选择首选的云提供商，我决定使用 AWS，因为我已经有一些使用它的经验，并且最近完成了 AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner 考试。

由于我选择使用 AWS，我开始布置完成项目所需的基础设施。 创建访客计数器将涉及 JavaScript 调用 API Gateway，触发 Python Lambda 函数，该函数将读取和附加 DynamoDB 表，并且将使用 IAM 管理必要的角色。 最终，基础架构将需要编写为“基础架构即代码”或 IaC。

执行

根据我做 AWS 实验室的经验，我使用了 AWS 的一些单独部分，但没有达到这个项目所需的水平。 因此，我决定从手动设置 S3 静态网站、Route 53 和 CloudFront 分配开始。 虽然一切正常，但我了解到任何代码更改都需要使 CloudFront 缓存失效以传播新代码。

我目前正在处理显示访客计数器正确更新的错误。 我在 IaC 方面也有进展，我一直在 Terraform 工作，并且有很多错误需要解决。 更多即将到来。



原文:

I have been working in Operational Technology (industrial control systems in power plants and oil refineries) for about 15 years, doing everything from initial construction and commissioning, to maintenance and modernization design reviews. I recently decided to transition to information security, and through studying and implementing projects, I fell in love with cloud services. This discovery pushed me to begin studying AWS services, which led me to find Cloud Resume Challenge.

Background

The project is a challenge that was created by Forrest Brazeal, who works in DevOps and cloud engineering. The challenge is to build a build a simple, static website of their resume using a multitude of cloud services. As the challenge allows you to choose your preferred cloud provider, I decided to used AWS because I already had some experience using it already, and had recently completed the AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner exam.

As I had chosen to use AWS, I began to lay out the infrastructure that would be needed to complete the project. S3 would host the static website, Route 53 and CloudFront would manage DNS requirements and secure website distribution. Creating a visitor counter would involve JavaScript calling API Gateway, triggering a Python Lambda function, which would read and append a DynamoDB table, and the necessary Roles would be managed with IAM. Eventually, the infrastructure would need to be written as "Infrastructure as Code" or IaC.

Implementation

In my experience doing AWS labs, I had used some individual parts of AWS, but not to the level that this project would demand. Because of this, I decided to start by manually setting up a S3 static website, Route 53, and CloudFront distribution. While everything went fine, I learned that any change of code necessitated an invalidation of the CloudFront cache to propagate the new code.

I am currently working on an error displaying the visitor counter updating correctly. I also have progress to make in the IaC front, I have been working in Terraform and have many bugs to work out. More to come soon.
